title: Training // Track 1 - Snowflake GenAI Essentials Training
description: Spend the day learning how to turn your data into intelligent, AI-powered results with gen AI on Snowflake. Build, prompt and deploy real generative AI solutions to deliver measurable impact, all in a secure, scalable, environment.

title: Training // Track 2 - Snowflake Accelerator Training
description: Jumpstart your platform skills with this one-day Summit course for early to intermediate users. We'll move beyond theory with a hands-on exploration of curated, key features, from core tools to new AI. You'll leave with practical skills from Snowflake experts to solve complex problems and immediately unlock more value.

title: Training // Track 3 - Snowflake Platform Training + Exam - Monday
description: This session is designed for individuals with limited experience using Snowflake and provides a walkthrough of the AI Data Cloud architecture through interactive labs, guiding learners how to unify, integrate and share previously siloed data with virtually unlimited scalability and concurrency.

title: Accelerating AI Readiness: Data to Real-Time Answers with Snowflake Intelligence
description: This session shows how Daimler Truck North America accelerated AI readiness in manufacturing by placing Snowflake Intelligence at the center of their data platform. They ingest real-time data from enterprise and mainframe systems using Snowpipe Streaming and Kafka to enable low-latency operational visibility. Pipelines are orchestrated with dbt, with Dynamic Tables automating CDC and delivering near real-time datasets. As streaming scaled, they applied Dynamic Table best practices and Gen2 warehouses to control costs. We conclude by demonstrating how Snowflake Intelligence, powered by semantic models and Cortex Analyst, enables teams to ask real-time operational questions to move beyond dashboards.

title: Agent Monday: Automated, Personalized Shopper Insights at Scale
description: What if every stakeholder received curated, actionable shopper insights — automatically? Meet Agent Monday, an AI-powered agent that synthesizes behavioral data and delivers tailored insights directly to inboxes. Explore the architecture, personalization logic and governance guardrails that turn raw retail data into decision-ready intelligence at scale.

title: Automated Data Classification and Pseudonymization for Securely Sharing Data
description: Struggling with manual data governance? Learn how to build an enterprise-grade data pseudonymization framework leveraging Snowflake native capabilities. Join Snowflake Data Superhero Chris Hastie as he demonstrates automated data classification and tag-based dynamic data masking for policy-driven protection and secure sharing of pseudonymized data. Walk away with production-ready patterns for automated PII detection across your estate, reusable masking policies that adapt to tag values and real-time secure sharing with context-aware access control. Transform weeks of manual work into automated, scalable solutions that maintain analytical utility while ensuring privacy compliance.
room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

title: Aviva's Internal Marketplace Playbook: From 29 Silos to One Source of Truth
description: With 29 business units and hundreds of disparate data products, Aviva faced a fragmented landscape that hindered cross-organizational agility. While an initial migration to Snowflake delivered 60% cost savings, the true challenge remained: scaling a consistent operating model. This session reveals how Aviva is using Snowflake Internal Marketplace to consolidate these silos into a single source of truth. By industrializing data products, Aviva has enabled rapid prototyping and high-velocity collaboration across the group. Learn how this shift from fragmentation to a unified marketplace is pivotal to reaching Aviva's £2 billion profit target and fostering a culture of continuous innovation.

title: Build an Iceberg Lakehouse with Snowflake and Microsoft Fabric OneLake
description: As data volumes increase and AI workloads proliferate, copying and ETL-ing data across systems is no longer sustainable. This hands-on workshop walks through building a fully interoperable, open lakehouse by integrating Snowflake and Microsoft OneLake. Learn how to leverage Apache Iceberg™ to create an open, governed data foundation that allows both Snowflake and Microsoft Fabric engines to operate on the same datasets without data duplication or complex pipelines. You will use sample data in Fabric, create an external volume and set up Microsoft OneLake as a catalog-linked database in Snowflake, create Iceberg tables in OneLake and query them both from Snowflake and Fabric.

title: Cortex AI Functions: Unstructured Data in Manufacturing and Customer Analytics
description: Unlock the power of your unstructured data by turning manual bottlenecks into high-speed engines for growth. In this session, we explore how Snowflake Cortex AI revolutionized two critical workflows: manufacturing verification and customer sentiment analysis. By leveraging Cortex AI Functions and Streamlit, we slashed document verification times from 340 minutes to just 10 (a 97% reduction) and accelerated feedback parsing by 90%. Join us to learn how to transform complex PDFs and subjective comments into objective, AI-ready assets that drive faster, more strategic decision-making.

title: Driving Continuous Price-Performance Optimization at T-Mobile with Snowflake
description: Optimization isn’t a one-time event — it’s a continuous discipline. T-Mobile shares how they embedded telemetry-driven decision-making, automated cost controls and workload segmentation to sustain price-performance improvements over time. Learn how to operationalize optimization as an ongoing capability.

title: From Data Sharing to Data Products: A Governed Marketplace in the AI Data Cloud
description: As enterprises shift from ad hoc data sharing to reusable data products, data governance becomes vital. This session explores how Infosys enables a governed, data product marketplace on the Snowflake AI Data Cloud, embedding AI and responsible AI principles across data, models and consumption. Learn how standardized policies, automated guardrails and lifecycle governance ensure trust and compliance. Discover how organizations scale data sharing securely and unlock greater value through AI-driven use cases.
room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

title: Future-Ready by Design: Boston Scientific’s Self-Service D&A Op Model
description: In medical devices, product parity is the norm. Competitive advantage comes from how fast you turn data into decisions. In this session, learn how phData and Boston Scientific partnered to transform the company's cardiology division by building a self-service data and analytics operating model with Snowflake at the center, turning a fragile IT relationship into a true business-IT fusion team that shifted data product ownership directly to the business. The result: Thousands of users now have trusted, timely insights built on a durable, scalable op model ready for AI.
room: Basecamp South Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

title: How DraftKings Ensures Regulatory Compliance at Scale Using Snowflake
description: DraftKings operates in one of the most heavily regulated digital industries, where data accuracy, lineage and timeliness are nonnegotiable. In this session, we’ll share how DraftKings designed its data architecture to meet the demands of operating across multiple highly regulated jurisdictions, with Snowflake as the core platform that enables scale and consistency. We’ll walk through how data is ingested, standardized and transformed, how compute is orchestrated, and how Snowflake’s native security, governance via Horizon Catalog, and observability features enable consistent, repeatable compliance across all our jurisdictions.

title: How SPS Commerce Scaled a Multimillion-Dollar Analytics Business with Sigma
description: SPS Commerce is reinventing its embedded analytics platform, SPS Analytics, by replacing legacy BI with Sigma on Snowflake. The platform helps retailers and partners unify sell-through, inventory and supplier data to improve forecasting, optimize inventory and drive stronger outcomes across the retail ecosystem. In this session, Troy Prothero, Senior Director of Product Management at SPS Commerce, shares why SPS replatformed its analytics platform and how Sigma enables collaborative workflows with writeback capabilities that allow users to refine operational data in Snowflake. Learn how this foundation is preparing SPS Commerce for the next generation of AI-powered applications.
room: Basecamp South Theater 4 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

title: How to Evaluate Snowflake Gen2 Safely in Production and the Outcomes at Scale
description: Upgrading core infrastructure requires rigor. This session outlines a controlled framework for evaluating Snowflake Gen2 in live production environments — covering A/B validation, workload benchmarking and risk mitigation. Hear measurable outcomes and lessons learned from enterprise-scale deployment.
room: Basecamp South Theater 3 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

title: Designing a Low-Latency Warehouse for 1.5M Daily Queries
description: Serving 1.5 million daily queries demands architectural precision. This session breaks down workload isolation, clustering strategy, caching optimization and data modeling patterns used to achieve consistently low latency at scale. Learn the trade-offs and engineering decisions that enabled sub-second performance under heavy concurrency.
room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

title: DRIVING INNOVATION VIA DIGITAL SUPPLY NETWORKS IN A CLINICAL SUPPLY CHAIN
description: Clinical trial supply chains are reaching their limits under growing complexity, fragmented data and reactive operating models. This session introduces the Digital Supply Network (DSN). DSN represents a shift from linear supply chains to a real-time, interconnected system that senses, predicts and responds to change. Powered by AI agents across demand, supply, logistics and compliance, the DSN enables proactive decision-making, continuous optimization and exception-based human oversight. The result is a more resilient, efficient and patient-focused supply model, ensuring the right medication reaches the right patient at the right time.

title: Enforcing Data Governance at the Orchestration Layer
description:
Financial institutions don't struggle with governance frameworks. They struggle with enforceability. When regulators ask how an AI decision was produced, most teams cannot prove which data ran, which controls were applied or who approved exceptions. Governance on paper is not governance at runtime.

This session shows how leading institutions use orchestration to codify policy, enforce controls, capture audit trails and gate high-risk decisions before they reach agents. We'll ground this in real-world patterns from financial institutions using Airflow and Astro to bring governance and control to their most complex data pipelines.
room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

title: From Data to Outcomes: How Ecolab Uses Snowflake ML to Deliver Customer Value
description: Imagine every customer location performing at its best — every shift, every day. In this session, hear how Ecolab turned raw operational data into ML models that show exactly what each site needs to reach peak performance. We'll take you inside the journey: where the spark began, how teams pushed through complexity to find signal in the noise, and the moment explainable AI began revealing actionable growth opportunities hiding in plain sight. See how predictive modeling, fast iteration and a shared obsession with customer outcomes translated into stronger relationships, measurable revenue gains and a new blueprint for AI-driven value creation.

title: How Fanatics Manages dbt Metrics with Cortex Code for Natural Language Analytics
description: In this session, Christine Kwon, Senior Director of Data at Fanatics, and Brett Pendleton, Manager of Analytics Engineering at Fanatics, share how they used dbt to define and govern Fanatics’ metric source of truth using scalable, metadata-driven models (YAML-based definitions, testing and documentation). They will walk through how those curated models power Snowflake semantic views and Snowflake Intelligence, enabling business users to ask trusted, governed questions in natural language.

title: How LendingTree Marketers Built a Snowflake Agent for Smarter SEM Decisions
description: Marketing teams waste hours digging through campaign data to find actionable insights. See how LendingTree, with the help of BlueCloud, transformed its search engine marketing with an AI agent that instantly surfaces performance trends, identifies optimization opportunities and recommends high-impact actions — cutting analysis time dramatically. A LendingTree marketer will demonstrate how the team built an intelligent assistant connecting Google Ads performance with customer data, enabling faster, data-driven decisions without waiting on analysts. Learn how this AI-powered approach is driving measurable campaign ROI improvements and freeing marketers to focus on strategy rather than spreadsheets.

title: Modernizing Data Security: How Ally Financial Scaled Native Classification
description: Discover how Ally Financial modernized data security by migrating from third-party solutions to Snowflake Horizon Catalog's native data classification capabilities. In this session, we’ll share our journey to scaling classification across the enterprise, enhancing data protection and compliance. Learn about the challenges faced, key implementation strategies and measurable improvements in security management. Gain practical insights to help your organization streamline and strengthen data security using Snowflake’s native tools.
room: Basecamp South Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

title: Self-Healing Semantic Layers with Snowflake Cortex AI: How Sigma Built J.A.K.E.
description: Sigma's data platform team built J.A.K.E., a production-grade agent powered by Cortex AI that integrates directly into Slack and Sigma, letting teams interact with Snowflake in their everyday work environment. This session showcases a self-healing reference architecture that actively maintains dbt metadata and dimensional business matrices. By converting modeling frameworks into machine-executable logic, J.A.K.E. automatically generates Snowflake semantic views, eliminating weeks of manual effort. Attendees will see live AgenticOps examples: using Cortex AI to capture query failures, debug root causes in real time and automatically patch semantic definitions to create a continuous learning loop.

title: The Art of Data Relationship Chemistry for Global Financial Services
description: Global financial services firms operate where regulation, capital and trust collide. For private equity and their investors, unlocking tax value depends on understanding relationships across entities, partners, deals and jurisdictions — yet the data is often trapped in spreadsheets. In this session, we explore how a PE-backed global firm is rebuilding its tax data foundation on Snowflake, turning fragmented records into a connected, insight-driven platform that unlocks transparency, allows risk analysis to drive decisions and cuts review hours in half.
room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

title: Zero-Copy, Bidirectional Integrations: SAP, Salesforce, Workday and Beyond
description: Traditional integrations require costly data replication and create operational lag. In this session, we will demonstrate how Snowflake Zero-Copy, Bidirectional Integration can archive near real-time connectivity between Snowflake and enterprise applications without data movement. We will showcase implementations with Salesforce, Workday and more to explore how we maintain data consistency across systems.

title: AI-Powered FinOps Transformation at Ally Financial
description: Ally Financial revolutionized its financial operations (FinOps) strategy by integrating advanced AI capabilities through Snowflake Intelligence and Cortex Analyst. This session details how the company moved beyond traditional cost management to implement a proactive, AI-driven optimization framework within the Snowflake platform. We will showcase specific use cases and methodologies where Snowflake Cortex AI Analyst provided actionable insights, leading to improved resource utilization and significant cost savings. Attendees will learn how to leverage these native Snowflake AI features to modernize their own FinOps practices and drive better business outcomes.
room: Basecamp South Theater 3 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

title: AI-Powered Modernization: Oregon's Multnomah County Leverages SnowConvert AI
description: Public sector organizations are under pressure to modernize legacy systems, control costs and deliver better services for their communities. Multnomah County — home to Portland, Oregon — faced these same challenges as it sought to move away from expensive, aging SQL Server infrastructure. Together with Snowflake, the county leveraged SnowConvert AI to accelerate code conversion and migration, transforming its data operations onto the Snowflake Data Cloud. Join us to hear how this partnership reduced costs, improved scalability and positioned the county to deliver smarter, data-driven services to citizens through Snowflake Intelligence.

title: Building a Trusted AI Data Foundation with Salesforce and Snowflake
description: Discover how the strategic partnership between Salesforce and Snowflake creates a unified architecture to unlock the full potential of the agentic enterprise. Attendees will learn how current integrations across Tableau, Data 360 and Agentforce enable humans and agents to drive success together.
room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

title: Deploying Geospatial Solutions via Natural Language with Cortex Code
description: This session demonstrates how to transform natural language requests into deployment-ready geospatial applications using Cortex Code. You will learn how to parameterize solution templates and extend logic to support organization-specific KPIs. Discover how Snowflake enables businesses to optimize delivery routes, increase fleet utilization, and reduce both idle time and operational costs. Attendees will gain practical knowledge on using Cortex Code to deploy and customize geospatial solutions within the Snowflake native stack. All session materials, including code and templates, will be made publicly available for immediate use.
room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

title: ExxonMobil's Enterprise Data Transformation with Marketplace Capacity Drawdown
description: Discover how ExxonMobil transformed its data landscape from 7,000 fragmented data products scattered across 20 legacy platforms into a single, enterprisewide central data hub. Learn how it leveraged the Snowflake Marketplace Capacity Drawdown (MCD) program to consolidate 350 governed data products powering more than 250 use cases while providing flexible payment options across multiple lines of business. This session covers practical strategies for large-scale data modernization, including managing complex budget structures across business units, derisking multiyear platform investments and using MCD to accelerate partner tool adoption.

title: Indeed’s Playbook: Auto-Scaling APIs, Lower TCO and Fast Queries on Snowflake
description: Employer analytics is hard, with low-latency, high-concurrency customer APIs plus compute-heavy batch refreshes of aggregates — without an infrastructure empire. This talk shares how Indeed's Employer Analytics group moved from self-managed Vertica to Snowflake Virtual Warehouses for all data access. Warehouses let our APIs auto-scale with traffic, delete years of ops scaffolding, free engineers for product work and cut TCO with lower compute costs (projected ~$2M/year savings). We'll also cover Interactive tables and Interactive warehouses, improving p95 latency ~30% and laying a foundation for next-gen agentic analytics.

title: Messy Pipelines Are Expensive: A Hygiene Framework for Cutting Snowflake Waste
description:
dbt’s dependency graphs make building and scaling data pipelines easier than ever, but hidden inefficiencies can quietly drive up costs. Redundant pipelines, oversized warehouses, forgotten workloads and slow queries can add up to serious Snowflake waste. 

In this session, you will learn a practical hygiene framework to identify and fix inefficiencies across your pipelines. We’ll also show you how autonomous agents can make this a continuous process, turning cleanup from a one-time chore into an ongoing advantage.
room: Basecamp South Theater 4 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

title: Regional Data Moves at Scale: Solving Data Locality Without Breaking Analytics
description: As global SaaS platforms scale, data locality and regional compliance become continuous operational requirements. Customer data must move across regions safely and predictably, while analytics pipelines remain consistent and trustworthy. In this session, we share how Zendesk designed a Snowflake-native approach for cross-regional account moves by aligning operational cutovers with explicit UTC day boundaries. By introducing a preemptive data-processing lock coordinated with upstream cutover guarantees, downstream layers process only fully closed days. The result is a predictable, low-maintenance pattern for regional data moves at scale.

title: Scaling AI with Cyera, Snowflake and Your Company's Ecosystem
description:
Enterprises are investing heavily in AI, but many initiatives stall before production — not due to models or compute, but because of fragmented data, poor visibility, and weak access controls that undermine trust and compliance.

Snowflake accelerates AI by centralizing data and enabling scalable access, but this speed increases risk as sensitive data proliferates across environments.

Cyera addresses this gap as an AI-native data security control plane, providing full visibility, automated classification, risk prioritization and policy enforcement across Snowflake ecosystems — enabling organizations to scale AI with trusted, governed and secure data.
room: Basecamp South Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

title: The Blueprint for Building SCM Intelligence for Perishable Business on Snowflake
description: Dominating 70% of the U.S. tofu market, Pulmuone unified more than 20,000 products by migrating fragmented ERP, SCM and WMS data to Snowflake. This session reveals how it achieved a real-time integrated view, syncing production and sales for instant S&OP analysis. By leveraging Snowflake, Pulmuone streamlined the entire supply chain — from manufacturing to CS. With AI-driven demand forecasting and trend analysis, the company reduced SCM costs by about 25%, boosted analyst productivity by 50% and improved forecast accuracy by 20%. Learn how Pulmuone transformed a complex global operation into a lean, data-driven engine, proving that Snowflake is the ultimate ingredient for scalable business growth.

title: Agentic ML in Action: Train, Deploy and Monitor Models with Cortex Code
description: What if you could go from a blank notebook to a monitored production model using just natural language? Cortex Code brings agentic AI to the Snowflake ML lifecycle by training models, engineering features with Snowflake Feature Store, registering and versioning models in Model Registry, deploying real-time inference endpoints on Snowpark Container Services, running batch predictions at scale with ML jobs, tracking experiments and more. In this session, we demo the full journey: explore data, train a model, build feature pipelines, deploy to a GPU endpoint and set up monitoring — all driven by an AI agent through conversation. See how agentic ML development collapses weeks of MLOps into minutes.

title: Building Real-Time, Multimodal AI Agents with Snowflake
description: Generative AI often sits isolated in experimental notebooks, disconnected from the high-velocity data of the real world. In this session, we introduce a Snowflake architecture (Snowflake, NiFi, Apache Iceberg, Cortex, Kafka) that includes a blueprint for operationalizing AI on data in motion. We will demonstrate how to move beyond static RAG by building a live "vision agent" that ingests real-time video streams, performs autonomous image analysis using multimodal LLMs (Cortex) and triggers downstream actions instantly. Attendees will see a live blueprint where data ingestion, vectorization and inference happen in a unified, serverless loop, turning raw chaos into structured intelligence.
room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

title: Context, Governance, Infrastructure: The Stack AI Data Agents Don't Have
description: AI agents can build pipelines, generate dbt models, evolve Snowflake schemas and write quality tests. But, they lack three pieces essential for production-grade data engineering demands: 1) context about your data stack and business, 2) governance to enforce access controls, policies and cost constraints, and 3) infrastructure to validate changes before they hit your warehouse. Pradnesh Patil, Co-Founder of Altimate.ai, shares the missing stack for agentic data engineering. Watch agents catch schema drift, self-correct hallucinated models and enforce guardrails in Snowflake and across the entire data platform.
speaker_title: Senior Director, Enterprise Data & AI Platform
room: Basecamp South Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

title: Leveraging AI-Powered Analytics to Manage the Guest Experience with Fogo de Chão
description: Fogo de Chão is an internationally renowned restaurant from Brazil that elevates the culinary art of churrasco into a cultural dining experience. Fogo offers a continuous selection of high-quality proteins carved tableside and access to a market table with a selection of seasonal salads, exotic fruit, superfoods and more. Managing guest satisfaction and operational excellence at scale requires more than intuition — it demands data. Fogo de Chão's partnership with Domo is a cornerstone of their data strategy, enabling real-time insights. Discover how Fogo de Chão leverages AI-powered analytics to manage reputation and drive operational efficiency to make smarter decisions every day.

title: Snowflake Intelligence for Trading and Risk Management
description: Learn how ofi (Olam Food Ingredients) drives its decision-making processes by using Snowflake Intelligence agents. The agents have been built for sales, hedging and procurement insights, enabling teams to move from static reporting to proactive, insight-led decisions. These agents operate on top of historical, curated enterprise datasets combined with semantic data models, ensuring that insights are generated with consistent business context and shared definitions. As a result, they empower decision‑makers with timely, contextual and actionable insights — supporting better pricing and demand decisions in sales, more informed risk insights and improved optimization strategies in procurement.
room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

title: The AI-Ready Bank: How Peoples Bank Unified Core Systems on Snowflake
description: Most regional banks run on fragmented systems — core banking, Salesforce, nCino — siloing customer data. As AI capabilities accelerate, banks without a unified data foundation risk missing the next wave of agentic automation and intelligence. Peoples Bank of Ohio, a $10 billion+ institution, partnered with Coastal to do something about it. It replaced a legacy SQL registry with Snowflake medallion architecture, used dbt to unify operational systems and turned its general ledger into a trusted customer 360. Get the blueprint: See how the bank delivered golden IDs and automated revenue monitoring and marketing segmentation via zero-copy integration while building an AI-ready foundation for Snowflake.

title: Agentic CRM: AI Driving Real Transformation
description: Learn about an agentic CRM experience to become sales reps’ sidekicks and remove the operational overhead of traditional CRM applications. The future of CRM is not another application that needs you to manually make updates; it is an enabler that handles all of your busy work while being your proactive and supportive assistant. Align your agentic, sales and data strategies by running Elementum on your Snowflake instance.
room: Basecamp South Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

title: AI-Powered Analytics and Data Discovery Using Snowflake at Booking.com
description: Commercial, product and platform teams — from partnerships, trips and accommodations – are using Snowflake’s AI capabilities to power self-service analytics and data discovery. Built on a multienvironment infrastructure, analytical semantics from multiple business units are integrated with Booking’s data discovery and low-code AI application, enabling teams to ask product, performance and data management contextual questions using natural language in a variety of contexts. Snowflake Intelligence, semantic views and agents are reducing our dependence on dashboards and data tribal knowledge — freeing up analysts’ time for complex experimentation, causal inference and strategic analysis.

title: Analytics to AI Apps: Democratizing Agentic Workflows with Sigma and Snowflake
description:
Stop looking at data and start acting on it. Top organizations empower employees across all functions and levels to build intelligent AI apps that interpret data, automate workflows and drive immediate action.

Join this hands-on lab to see how Sigma connects business users, live analytics and Snowflake Cortex Agents. Using a real-world retail loss prevention scenario, you'll replace legacy reporting with a custom AI app that runs Python machine learning and embeds Snowflake Cortex AI chat agents to investigate fraud in real time.

Walk away with a replicable blueprint for deploying code-free AI apps across your enterprise, no specialized engineering resources required.

title: Apache Iceberg™ v3 Tables in Snowflake: A Practical Guide
description: The release of Apache Iceberg™ v3 tables is a major step for open data architectures. But beyond a version number, what does v3 mean when managing an open lakehouse in Snowflake? This session introduces the Iceberg table format, its evolution and the key breakthroughs of v3. We’ll highlight practical benefits — better CDC support, more efficient handling of semi-structured data and improved metadata management — and show how these features help solve real-world lakehouse challenges. Through a series of live demos, you’ll learn how to get started with v3 features in Snowflake so you can hit the ground running with this new format version.
room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

title: Building the Agentic Enterprise with Informatica from Salesforce and Snowflake
description: Agentic transformation requires a foundation of unified, accessible and context-rich data. This session explores how Informatica, now part of the Salesforce Data Foundation, provides a framework to unlock, trust and activate high-fidelity context for building robust agentic workflows with Snowflake Cortex AI. Join us to explore data management strategies to unlock connectivity across disparate enterprise systems, build trust through automated quality and AI-ready governance, and activate insights to ground agents with reliable and relevant context. See how a real-world story of governed context accelerates agent deployment to power the agentic enterprise.

title: From 12 to 200: Scaling U.S. Bank’s Data Product Strategy with Snowflake
description: U.S. Bank is transforming data from a siloed asset into a scalable product, growing its internal marketplace from 12 to 200 AI-ready data products by 2027. This session explores how it's empowering domain teams to curate data products using Snowflake Internal Marketplace and Dynamic Tables, while aligning them with business outcomes such as revenue modeling and forecasting. Learn how the CDAO office uses "data ambassadors" to drive enterprise-wide adoption, and discover their roadmap for decentralizing data ownership while maintaining central governance.
speaker_title: VP of Data Platform Engineering Lead

title: Getting Your Data Cortex AI-Ready with Fivetran, Snowflake and dbt
description: In 90 minutes, you'll go hands-on building a production-ready AI chatbot — taking raw data from ingestion all the way to an experience powered by Snowflake Intelligence using Fivetran and dbt Platform. Guided step by step, this lab is designed for all skill levels. Whether you're new to the modern data stack or a seasoned practitioner, you'll walk away with working knowledge of Snowflake Cortex AI and a repeatable blueprint you can take back to your organization.

title: How Canva Built a Petabyte-Scale Optimization Ecosystem
description: Petabyte-scale environments require granular visibility and disciplined governance. This session explores cost attribution models, storage lifecycle management and compute segmentation strategies that drive accountability without slowing innovation. This session is ideal for large enterprises managing complex, multi-domain workloads.

title: Identifying Chronic Conditions Using Snowflake Cortex AI
description: Due to the high risk of their patient population and the strain on primary care physicians, chronic illnesses often go undiagnosed and untreated. Agilon's goal is to leverage LLMs and its massive patient data set to help uncover and diagnose such conditions, providing its patients a happier, healthier life. Agilon is utilizing its centralized clinical data with Snowflake Cortex AI to reason through millions of patient documents. This enables Agilon to instantly and accurately flag potential undiagnosed ICD-10 codes at scale for its 600,000-patient population. The financial uplift: Accurately capturing these missing codes is expected to drive roughly $100 million annually of incremental revenue.

title: Scaling Enterprise AI in a Multicloud Global Organization
description: Danaher, a global science and technology leader in life sciences, diagnostics and biotechnology, operates through multiple independent companies worldwide. As the organization scaled, data became fragmented across clouds, regions and systems. To unlock enterprisewide intelligence, Danaher built a multicloud, multiregion enterprise data platform on Snowflake using a data product-centric approach. Delivered with Capgemini, the platform enables secure cross-company data sharing, unified governance and AI-driven insights at scale. Today, it supports financial optimization, commercial intelligence and advanced scientific analytics, accelerating AI adoption across the enterprise.

title: See the Light: Migrating ETL to Snowflake with SnowConvert AI
description: ETL migration is complex. In this hands-on lab, you'll migrate a real ETL pipeline to Snowflake using SnowConvert AI. We'll demonstrate an agentic workflow, automated analysis, code conversion, AI verification, plus data migration and validation — so you can see what to automate. You'll work in SnowConvert AI and leave with a migration checklist.

title: SONY INTERACTIVE’S BLUEPRINT FOR UNIFIED DATA AND CATALOG FEDERATION AT SCALE
description: Sony Interactive Entertainment (SIE) is defining a new standard for technical leadership by embracing the open, interoperable data ecosystem. This visionary session frames catalog federation not as a future goal, but as a present necessity. SIE will detail the strategic architecture that leverages Snowflake’s Iceberg tables and Horizon Catalog-linked databases to ensure every table — regardless of its open format — is universally discoverable and accessible by all compute engines, with an emphasis on advanced security and governance. This comprehensive blueprint provides technical leaders with a clear, actionable path to deliver an open, innovative data platform at scale.

title: THE SNOWFLAKE WELL-ARCHITECTED FRAMEWORK: DESIGNING EFFICIENCY
description: A well-architected Snowflake environment balances agility, performance, security and cost efficiency. This session introduces the Snowflake Well-Architected Framework, outlining core design pillars, lenses and implementation patterns. Learn how to assess your environment and apply prescriptive guidance to drive sustainable platform excellence.

title: TRUST BY DESIGN: HOW TOYOTA MOTOR EUROPE GOVERNS DATA AND AI ON SNOWFLAKE
description: Toyota Motor Europe reveals how it uses the Snowflake AI Data Cloud to build the disciplined, trust-first foundations needed for responsible AI. Inspired by the principle that AI is “20% fun, 80% hard work,” Toyota shows how strong governance with Snowflake Horizon Catalog, GDPR alignment, consent management, and EU Data and AI Act readiness are embedded into every data product. Learn how Toyota’s governed data mesh and data marketplace democratize high-quality, compliant data so teams can innovate confidently and sustainably across the organization.

title: WHERE DATA MEETS INNOVATION: REDEFINING ENTERPRISE INTELLIGENCE
description: Ready to move beyond dashboards and give business users answers they can trust in seconds? Join NTT DATA to see how Snowflake Cortex and Snowflake Intelligence power an enterprise-scale cognitive analytics platform that unifies structured and unstructured data, dynamically governs the semantic layer, and accelerates decision-making.

title: BRIDGING THE GAP: POWERING REAL-TIME APPS WITH SNOWFLAKE AND POSTGRES
description: Managing a 6 TB install base in Snowflake is ideal for analytics, but how do you serve that same data to high-performance web applications? Ericsson faced this challenge: needing to move from massive analytical queries to sub-second, small-result lookups for UI users. Enter Snowflake Postgres. This session explores how we mirror 6 TB of data using advanced data movement capabilities to bridge the gap between the Data Cloud and the application layer. Learn how we achieve rapid UI responsiveness while ensuring all application changes flow seamlessly back into Snowflake for a unified, bidirectional data ecosystem.

title: BUILD A UNIFIED METADATA CATALOG WITH ICEBERG IN DOORDASH'S LAKEHOUSE PLATFORM
description: Join us as DoorDash shares their battle-tested blueprint for turning Iceberg interoperability into a repeatable, production-grade lakehouse platform. Get insights from their real-world journey adopting Apache Iceberg as the lakehouse table format. Learn about query performance, catalog latency and data optimization, including VARIANT, deletion vectors, CDF/CDC patterns, operationalization via Snowflake catalog-linked databases and Iceberg table replication. We'll cover the “gotchas” of multi-engine architecture patterns, including feature parity gaps, type/schema mismatches and SQL behavior differences.

title: CONTEXT IS THE NEW CODE: AI ENGINEERING WITH SNOWFLAKE CORTEX AI
description: As AI becomes capable of writing SQL and building data pipelines, the real bottleneck is no longer code — it’s context. Many enterprise AI initiatives struggle not because the models are weak, but because they lack the business logic, definitions and operational nuance that reside within human expertise. In this session, we’ll explore a practical approach to encoding both business and technical context so Snowflake Cortex AI can generate reliable, enterprise-grade data workflows. The result: AI that doesn’t just write code, but operates like a true enterprise data engineer.

title: DATA GOVERNANCE TO AI INNOVATION: CAMDEN BANK ROI WITH QLIK CORTEX IN FS AI
description: Camden National Bank is launching Snowflake Cortex AI to cut insight cycles from weeks to minutes and deliver real-time customer growth insights for executives and frontline teams. But the bank started out on Snowflake with a data team of two. Learn how Camden National Bank modernized its platform using Snowflake on AWS and Qlik, using Passerelle’s Data Rocket to migrate over 600 million records. Hear how it built success, including a 250% increase in analytics output, certified data marts validating 99% of enterprise data and efficiencies that freed resources and funded new analytics roles. Then, discover how Camden National Bank is operationalizing Snowflake Intelligence across the organization.

title: DYNAMIC TABLES DEEP DIVE
description: Dynamic Tables is the new default for change data and incremental processing. Join us to learn about the latest features and innovations powering Snowflake’s declarative data pipelines — breaking the boundaries between batch and streaming pipelines. This session will provide an overview of Dynamic Tables and explore its key benefits, top use cases, latest improvements and newest capabilities. It will also feature a customer sharing their experience deploying Dynamic Tables and exploring the latest innovations.

title: HOW DTCC CREATED A CULTURAL SHIFT AROUND PROACTIVE VISIBILITY AND GOVERNANCE
description: DTCC, a leading financial market infrastructure company, depends on data to support securities clearing and settlement. Managing large cloud data sets can make spend visibility and forecasting difficult. This session explains how DTCC improved insight into Snowflake usage and costs, set proactive alerts for spikes, unified data into a single view, and fostered a culture of monitoring and optimization. We’ll share best practices and how DTCC improved unit economics across 87% of workloads.

title: HOW WHOOP USES CORTEX CODE AS AN AI CONTROL PLANE FOR AGENTIC INTELLIGENCE
description: WHOOP's use of Cortex Code has quickly improved how it builds and operates AI across Snowflake, from day-to-day development to the production-grade agents it delivers to its teams. Cortex Code operates as the AI control plane by incorporating WHOOP's enterprise data and context alongside AI models and applications in a fully governed manner, translating intelligence into authorized enterprise action. This has accelerated how WHOOP turns knowledge into usable AI experiences for all employees, both business and technical, while maintaining operational rigor across the company.

title: SIEMENS: SCALING ENTERPRISE DATA SHARING WITH SNOWFLAKE
description: At Siemens, we're building the future of industrial AI by enabling data sharing, discovery and consumption at scale. This session shares our journey in building the Siemens Data and AI Cloud, a unified data ecosystem powered by Snowflake with a data mesh architecture. We'll outline our strategic vision and technical foundations, explaining how federated data domains publish data as products for thousands of consumers to access trusted data. Learn how we democratize data access through the Siemens Data Cloud Marketplace, reducing time-to-value and transforming collaboration. We'll explore how this scalable, governed data-sharing approach accelerates industrial AI.

title: THE CONVERGED ENTERPRISE: CLOSING THE GAP BETWEEN SAP OPS AND AI INTELLIGENCE
description: SAP enterprises have invested billions in operational data. Most cannot activate that data for AI at scale, not because the technology is missing, but because two organizations own the problem and neither owns the seam between them.

title: THE INFRASTRUCTURE BEHIND GRINDR’S AI TRANSFORMATION
description: Grindr serves 15 million monthly active users across matching, insights, recommendations and safety — all powered by AI. Getting there required one key infrastructure decision: computing features at inference time, not in advance. In this session, Grindr's CPO, AJ Balance, and Chalk's CEO, Marc Freed-Finnegan, break down the Snowflake and Chalk stack that’s powering Grindr’s AI (aka gAI pronounced “gay-eye”) and what it means to ship real AI products at consumer scale for a global community of tens of millions.

title: TURN YOUR DATA INTO REVENUE: HOW TO ACHIEVE REAL-TIME MARKETING AND AI
description: Want more impact from your data team without adding tools, people or complexity? Learn how Fivetran Activations turns analytics-ready Snowflake data into real-time action. Operationalize trusted data for LLM workflows, power efficient go-to-market motions and personalize marketing, all without manual work. Hear how BraunAbility scaled AI across the U.S. and Europe by unifying data from multiple ERPs and Salesforce instances, simplifying their stack, accelerating integrations and empowering revenue teams with timely insights.

title: AGENTIC DATA ACCESS: BOOKING.COM’S AGENTIC TRAVEL EXPERIENCE, SECURED BY IMMUTA
description: AI agents are becoming top-level data consumers, and they need governed access at machine speed. In this session, Booking.com and Immuta share how Booking is extending Snowflake to support both humans and agents across structured and unstructured data in Snowflake and S3. You’ll learn how policy-driven provisioning, request-based workflows and an on-behalf-of access model help scale secure, auditable access without slowing down analytics or AI innovation.
speakers: Steve Touw Touw; Abhro Bhaduri Bhaduri

title: A PRACTICAL GUIDE TO BUILDING MARKETPLACE DATA PRODUCTS
description: Sharing data is harder than it should be. Permissions tell you who gets access, but they don't help people use the data effectively. For data to be truly valuable, you need to package it right. This session explores practical strategies for transforming raw data sets into compelling marketplace products. You'll learn how to bundle tables with context, create dashboards that tell a story and build assistants that understand your data. We'll cover publishing workflows for Snowflake Marketplace, real-world examples and ways to maintain control without bottlenecks. Whether sharing internally or building revenue streams via external sharing, learn to make your data valuable, not just available.

title: FROM CHAOS TO CONSISTENCY: USING SNOWFLAKE CORTEX TO STANDARDIZE FRAUD LABELS
description: At BioCatch, confirmed fraud labels provided by customers underpin our reporting, research and machine learning models. With over 200 customers across multiple regions and languages, these labels arrive in highly varied and inconsistent forms, creating significant operational challenges. In this session, we demonstrate how we use Snowflake Cortex AI in a multistage pipeline to automatically reclassify customer-provided fraud labels into standardized taxonomies. This approach enables scalable automation, improves downstream model training and introduces objective measures of label quality to help identify and address weaknesses in customer fraud processes.

title: GEICO'S JOURNEY TO UNIFIED ENTERPRISE LAKEHOUSE GOVERNANCE WITH HORIZON, POLARIS
description: GEICO, a leading insurer, struggled with data fragmentation across its multi-engine open-source lakehouse (Iceberg, Spark, Flink, Trino) and its EDW. Managing consistent security and governance across these disparate systems where Polaris served as the lakehouse catalog and Horizon governed the EDW created complexity. We'll showcase GEICO’s implementation of new Horizon features: full embedding of Polaris and support for IRC. This provides a single, universal governance layer, enforcing consistent FGAC, tagging and lineage across all data assets (OSS or Snowflake) achieving a true enterprise lakehouse, ensuring secure, multi-engine Iceberg access for AI-ready data discovery, without vendor lock-in.

title: GOLDMAN SACHS DATA LAKE: SCALABLE AND GOVERNED DATA DEMOCRATIZATION
description: Goldman Sachs launched its internal Data Lake platform on Snowflake to consolidate all existing data sets onto a common, governed data platform and ensure internal data democratization. The architecture is built on organizational listings, incorporating open-format tables, and leverages resharing to package external data into internal data products. This setup enables every team to easily discover and access data via Internal Marketplace. By connecting data from 54 providers to internal business teams, Goldman Sachs' data product setup ensures efficient data management and governance, accelerating enterprise-wide analytics and fostering a collaborative data ecosystem.

title: HOW CUSTOMERS ARE RUNNING AN EFFICIENT SNOWFLAKE ENVIRONMENT
description: This panel brings together real-world lessons from CrowdStrike, Zendesk and DTCC on how to maximize performance and economic value at scale in Snowflake. Panelists share battle-tested strategies for optimizing cost and performance using Gen2 warehousing, intelligent scaling, and query and data design best practices, resulting in millions in savings and faster, more real-time analytics.
speakers: Christine Avanessians; Mark Howerton; Anatoly Mikhaylov

title: HOW CYERA ENABLES SEMANTIC DATA LAYERS FOR ANALYTICS AND AI WITH SECURE SHARING
description: Modern SaaS products generate a flood of operational metadata that customers want to explore in BI — and now feed into AI. Instead of fragile CSV exports or one-off REST pulls, Snowflake Secure Data Sharing lets you publish governed, continuously updated data layers at scale for clients.

title: HOW NORWEGIAN CRUISE LINE OPTIMIZES FUEL, COMPLIANCE AND COSTS IN SNOWFLAKE ML
description: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH) built a first-of-its-kind, innovative Snowflake native fuel forecasting platform using Snowflake ML, unlocking millions of dollars in annual savings and strengthening regulatory compliance and procurement strategy. By unifying massive volumes of Internet of Things, routing and climatology data in Snowflake, NCLH trains and deploys ML models that predict fuel use for every ship, leg and port call with minute-by-minute granularity. The solution enables accurate procurement planning, reduced carbon credit exposure and near real-time insights — all powered end to end by Snowflake ML.

title: IS CONTEXT ENGINEERING THE NEW ANALYTICS ENGINEERING?
description: Analytics engineers built transformations to define metrics and share them in the data warehouse. But AI agents need more than definitions. They need context. At Checkr, the analytics engineering team treats context engineering as the next evolution of their practice. This session covers what it looks like to engineer context, organizational knowledge and governance into AI systems — including what semantic layers usually miss; how to accelerate with LLM-assisted tooling; how to build a feedback loop that makes AI smarter over time; and why this discipline is becoming the core of the analytics engineering role, not a side project.

title: OHIO TAX: CALL CENTER ANALYTICS, CALL TRANSCRIPTION AND AGENTIC WORKLOADS
description: The Ohio Department of Taxation administers most state and many local taxes for Ohio, including income, sales and business taxes. This year, it's standing up a Snowflake environment in Commercial Gov to run a Snowflake Cortex AI POC focused on call-center call quality analytics and a taxpayer chatbot, ahead of full FedRAMP High feature parity. Expected outcomes are faster, more consistent taxpayer support, better insight into call drivers and a reusable AI pattern for future tax and fraud analytics.
speaker_company: State of Ohio Department of Tax

title: TURN CONVERSATION INTO AI PIPELINES WITH DATAIKU AND SNOWFLAKE
description: What if AI development started with natural language collaboration instead of being code-first? With Dataiku’s Cobuild and Snowflake Cortex AI, natural language prompts can generate visual pipelines and production-ready AI workflows directly on Snowflake. Join this session to see how organizations enable data teams and domain experts to co-build AI applications faster. Discover how enterprises are expanding who can build AI while maintaining the governance, performance and scalability required by CIOs, CAIOs and CDOs.

title: OPENING KEYNOTE
description: Start Snowflake Summit 26 with a look at what’s next. Snowflake's CEO, Sridhar Ramaswamy, shares how organizations are turning AI’s promise into real business transformation and how the Snowflake AI Data Cloud is enabling a new era of enterprise intelligence and agentic innovation with data at the center. Joined by industry leaders and luminaries, he explores how agentic AI is already reshaping global industries and the critical challenges and opportunities facing today’s top business leaders.

title: BASECAMP WELCOME RECEPTION
description: Kick off Snowflake Summit with the Snowflake AI Data Cloud community. Meet peers, exchange ideas and start building connections in a welcoming setting designed to bring everyone together from the start.

title: 2026 DATA DRIVER AWARDS
description: The Data Drivers Awards is an annual awards program celebrating Snowflake customers (individuals and organizations) that are pushing the boundaries, driving innovation and delivering impact within their business and industry — using the Snowflake AI Data Cloud.

title: AI-Powered Conversations with Your Data Using Amazon Quick and Snowflake via MCP
description: The enterprise data landscape is scattered. Teams are trapped in disconnected tools, business users wait days for insights, and engineers build fragile one-off integrations. This hands-on workshop shows how to integrate Amazon Quick MCP actions with Snowflake Cortex AI's MCP server to build AI agents that transform how teams interact with enterprise data. Business users can ask questions such as "What are our top-performing products this quarter?" without writing SQL, while IT maintains security and governance controls. Walk out with working code and production-ready patterns that support self-service analytics across your organization.

title: DEPLOYING GEOSPATIAL SOLUTIONS USING CORTEX CODE SKILLS
description: Discover how Cortex Code skills can transform complex geospatial deployments into guided, repeatable workflows. This session demonstrates a real-world Fleet Intelligence solutions for retail, manufacturing, and travel and hospitality that perform sophisticated geospatial analytics, all deployed through an AI-assisted skill. Attendees will learn how to package domain expertise into reusable skills that handle template variables, validate prerequisites, execute idempotent SQL and provide self-healing error recovery, making advanced geospatial solutions accessible to any Snowflake user.

title: SECURE BY DEFAULT: IMPLEMENTING ZERO-TRUST IDENTITY AND ACCESS IN SNOWFLAKE
description: Managing static credentials and complex role hierarchies remains a leading cause of security incidents and operational fatigue. This 90-minute hands-on lab provides a deep dive into the next generation of Snowflake Identity and Access Management (IAM). Participants will gain hands-on experience with:

title: Platform Keynote
description: See how to move beyond AI experimentation and deliver measurable results in this keynote with Christian Kleinerman, Executive Vice President of Product at Snowflake. You’ll discover how Snowflake is redefining what’s possible with the Snowflake AI Data Cloud — and what that means for your organization. Hear directly from Snowflake leaders and customers transforming their businesses, and learn how to unify your data, govern AI with confidence and deploy secure, scalable agents that turn insight into action — making AI real for business at every scale.

title: Achieving Agentic AI ROI with Snowflake: Is Your Data Ready?
description: As organizations build AI agents and autonomous workflows on Snowflake, many struggle to turn AI investment into real ROI. The challenge isn’t the AI — it’s the data. A growing gap in data trust limits what autonomous systems can reliably deliver. In this session, we break down the six data challenges that hold agentic AI back — and discuss what it takes to prepare your Snowflake data for agent-driven outcomes. You’ll also see a demonstration of how Precisely is applying AI-driven automation and AI agents — delivered through interoperable services, APIs and data — to help accelerate the path to agentic-ready data for AI powered by Snowflake.
room: Basecamp South Theater 4 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

title: AI Agents and Forward Deployed Engineering for Commercial Decision Intelligence
description: Learn about Jarvis, Sanofi's AI sales adviser, bridging the gap between leadership strategy and field execution to drive a $1.5 billion annual revenue uplift. Powered by Snowflake Cortex Code, Cortex Search, Cortex Agents and SPCS, this Forward Deployed Engineering (FDE) engagement enables Sanofi to scale their commercial field force effectiveness agent across the globe for more than 10,000 employees. The system uses a hub-and-spoke model for global scale for deterministic compliance, ensuring data-driven clinical influence with zero human-in-the-loop.

title: Building Multiparty Intelligence with AI-Native Data Clean Rooms
description: The data you need lives in someone else's warehouse. This session provides the practical playbook for the fix. See how advertisers, publishers, healthcare networks and financial institutions are collaborating today: multiple parties, one neutral room, no exposed raw data. From setup to first result takes one business day, with partners already on Snowflake. We demo an overlap analysis with machine learning, then show how to get results out of the secure environment so you can actually use them, without leaving Snowflake's governance layer. No SQL required. Snowflake's agentic interface lets anyone configure a collaboration and run analysis in plain English.
room: Basecamp South Theater 3 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

title: Executive Lunch Hosted by dbt Labs
description: Hosted by dbt Labs, this partner-hosted lunch invites executive attendees to network and engage in conversation over a complimentary meal, available while supplies last.

title: How Eutelsat Uses Data and AI to Manage Global Connectivity
description: Running a satellite network isn’t like running a terrestrial one: coverage is global, demand is volatile and orbital resources are finite. Eutelsat uses data and AI to build a unified intelligence layer that orchestrates satellite traffic across continents and ground stations. Forecasting anticipates spikes, optimizes capacity and latency, and uses analytics to strengthen resilience and sustainability — turning satellites into adaptive infrastructure for aviation, maritime, enterprise and government. This session shows how to manage at planetary scale, ensure reliability and convert insight into services and revenue.
room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

title: Itron Powerflow for Grid Planning on Snowflake ML
description: Itron is redefining grid management by delivering structure-level Powerflow analysis for the world’s largest energy utilities. Electrification is fundamentally reshaping demand. Learn how Itron is using Snowflake ML to generate granular, high-frequency insight across both load and generation to maintain reliability, manage congestion and control emissions.

title: Move Fast, Don't Break Trust: New Developer Blueprint for AI-Driven Governance
description: Governance is often a finish line hurdle that slows delivery. This session introduces developer-native governance using Snowflake Cortex Code to move guardrails upstream. Learn how to embed AI-assisted peer reviews directly into Snowflake Workspaces, grounding LLMs in Snowflake Horizon Catalog metadata — including schemas, tags and masking policies. We demonstrate a "shift-left" workflow where Cortex identifies gaps and generates compliant DDL in real time. See how to automate policy mapping, produce human-readable audit justifications and reduce manual reviews by 60%. Transform governance from a bottleneck into an intelligent assistant for scalable, trusted data engineering.
room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

title: Open Data Infrastructure: The Next Era of Data Architecture
description: The modern data stack solved for siloed analytics, but AI and operational workloads demand a more adaptable foundation. In this thought leadership session, Fivetran explores open data infrastructure, an evolution beyond the modern stack that separates storage from compute and applies open standards across layers. Learn how to preserve interoperability, avoid lock-in, and scale analytics and AI with control over cost and architecture — then see ODI brought to life in a live demo.

title: Sharing Data and AI on Snowflake Internal Marketplace
description: Join this lab for a hands-on experience of sharing, requesting and consuming data and AI assets within your organization. You will share and govern not only basic data products including Apache Iceberg but also semantic models, AI agents and other artifacts. This lab teaches you how to publish such assets on Internal Marketplace, define governance controls and request and gain access. You will also exercise the latest Internal Marketplace features such as custom data product attributes to tailor the marketplace experience to your requirements.

title: Streaming into Interactive Tables with Interactive Warehouses
description: In this session, you will learn how to build a real-time streaming solution based on Interactive tables and Interactive warehouses. You will develop and deploy an end-to-end solution using Python, Snowpipe Streaming High-Performance Architecture and other data engineering features, which ingests data, transforms it and queries in seconds.

title: Structured Data Sharing for Government: From Secure Sharing to Clean Rooms
description: In Massachusetts state government, cross-agency analytics is as much a governance and legal challenge as a data one. This session shows how ODIA is enabling structured data sharing across agencies keeping data under agency stewardship while supporting enterprise insights. We’ll cover a practical progression on Snowflake: secure internal data sharing, discovery and reuse via listings, and purpose-bound collaboration with Clean Rooms for aggregate-only analysis. You’ll leave with a repeatable pattern for safer, auditable, revocable cross-agency analytics without centralizing sensitive data.
room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

title: The Future of HCLS: Modern Strategies for Interoperability and Agentic AI
description:
Navigating the next wave of interoperability and AI disruption across healthcare and life sciences means organizations need to ensure they are starting with the right foundational strategy, prioritizing the right use cases and demonstrating tangible ROI.
Join the healthcare and life sciences keynote at Summit 2026 to hear directly from industry innovators on how they are partnering with Snowflake to create the right strategy to tap into their multimodal data, accelerate interoperability, unlock enterprise AI and solve their most critical business challenges. We’ll discuss the latest Snowflake announcements and what they mean for the industry and show a live demo of Snowflake Intelligence.
speakers: Hiren Thakkar; Arun Verghese; Jeff Morrison
speaker_company: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts

title: The Future of Retail and Travel: Architecting for the Agentic Era with Snowflake
description: Agentic commerce is the next wave of AI disruption in retail and travel that demands organizations to start with a solid technology foundation and focus on the right priorities. Join this retail and travel industry keynote at Snowflake Summit to hear directly from the leading innovators on how they leverage Snowflake and our partner ecosystem to solve business challenges, accelerate technical use cases and unlock the power of enterprise agentic AI. Whether you’re a business executive, a technology leader or a Snowflake practitioner, this session is a must-attend for your Snowflake Summit experience.
speakers: Ravi Kota; Jeff Greenfield; Rosemary DeAragon
speaker_title: Sr. Director - Digital Consumer Data, AI

title: Trusting OT Data in Snowflake: How Devon Energy Ensures PI Data Quality at Scale
description: Devon Energy is migrating large volumes of PI historian data to Snowflake to support analytics, AI and reporting. Ensuring data quality — completeness, timeliness and accuracy — at scale is a key challenge. Manual checks don’t scale, so Devon partnered with Aperio to automate quality assurance. Aperio’s machine learning models detect drift, flatlines, noise and anomalies at the source, while its consistency product validates data alignment between PI and Snowflake. This closed-loop system ensures trusted, high-quality OT data for downstream analytics and decision-making.
room: Basecamp South Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

title: What's New: Snowflake Intelligence and Cortex Agents for Real Business Impact
description: AI is only as powerful as the context it understands. In this session, discover what’s new in Snowflake Intelligence, the enterprise intelligence agent built to turn all your knowledge into trusted business decisions. Learn how organizations are moving beyond dashboards to uncover the critical “why,” grounded in governed enterprise data and shared business definitions. Discover how Snowflake Intelligence and Cortex Agents work together to move teams beyond static insights, enabling natural language intelligence, trusted reasoning and actionable workflows that help organizations analyze, act and drive outcomes with confidence.

title: AI Readiness at Siemens: From Governed Semantics to Scalable Agents on Snowflake
description: Discover how Siemens built a governed, enterprise-scale AI data stack on Snowflake to securely connect automated agents with core business data. This technical session dives into our version-controlled semantic layer that ensures data consistency, as well as our architecture for extending user-based access control to the AI agents themselves. We'll demonstrate a productive agent that combines "talk to data" with research tasks to automate complex business processes, providing a blueprint for building a scalable and secure foundation for enterprise AI.
room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

title: Booking.com's Data Travels: Platform Foundations to Agentic Analytics
description: Booking.com is on a mission to remove friction from travel and now from data. In this session, discover how a robust Snowflake data platform is laying the foundations for scalable, trusted analytics across the business. Learn why enabling true self-service insights has become critical to unlocking faster decisions and operational efficiency. Hear how early pioneers in customer service are bringing this vision to life, going live with ThoughtSpot’s agentic analytics to empower frontline teams with AI-driven answers in the flow of work. See how this new analytics operating model is turning trusted data into better employee experiences and more seamless journeys for travelers.

title: How a Universal Semantic Layer Provides Context for AI
description: As organizations deploy AI copilots and agents on enterprise data, a core challenge emerges: AI doesn’t understand business meaning. Metrics, hierarchies and policies are often scattered across BI tools, data platforms and application logic. Humans can spot inconsistencies —AI cannot. This session shows how a universal semantic layer on Snowflake provides the context AI needs. By defining business semantics once and routing AI queries through a semantic query engine, organizations can deliver governed, deterministic answers while extending existing BI semantics to power trusted AI experiences.
room: Basecamp South Theater 3 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

title: Interoperability without Complexity: Snowflake Storage for Iceberg Tables
description: Running Apache Iceberg at scale often means managing buckets, IAM policies and background jobs. Interoperable storage removes that burden. In this session, learn how Snowflake brings native table simplicity to open Iceberg data. Snowflake handles compaction, metadata cleanup, security and lifecycle management. Tables remain fully interoperable and accessible to external engines through IRC APIs in Horizon. See how teams can migrate Iceberg workloads, support multi-engine access, and reduce operational complexity and total cost compared to customer-managed storage alternatives.
room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

title: Modernizing Enterprise Business Intelligence: PowerBI to Snowflake
description: This case study demonstrates replacing Microsoft PowerBI with Snowflake AI Data Cloud, achieving 98.8% cost savings ($300K→$3.6K annually) with superior performance. The platform leverages Snowflake Cortex AI Analyst for natural language queries, intelligence agents for autonomous analysis, Cortex Search and AI Functions, delivering advanced analytics through open-source technologies (React, FastAPI). This modernization eliminates vendor lock-in and proves cloud-native AI platforms can replace traditional BI tools at a fraction of the cost while providing enhanced capabilities and flexibility.
room: Basecamp South Theater 4 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

title: NHS London: Transforming Healthcare for 10M Patients Through Data Sharing
description: London serves a population of 10 million patients. NHS OneLondon has embarked on an ambitious program with Snowflake to improve health outcomes for this population by enabling secure data sharing across NHS trusts, local authorities and research organizations. Learn how this program is breaking down data silos to improve patient outcomes and the health and well-being of London's citizens — and discover how these data-sharing patterns can be applied to your own multiparty collaboration challenges.

title: Powering Billions in Billing with Apache Iceberg on Snowflake at Datadog
description: At Datadog, revenue engineering operates in the red zone, where billing failures directly impact revenue. We process every byte of product usage data to power invoices and internal analytics in Snowflake. For years, separate batch and streaming pipelines produced similar but inconsistent datasets, forcing downstream reconciliation. Analytics workloads querying this data through external tables at our scale led to expensive full scans and slow dashboards. In this talk, we show how we adopted Apache Iceberg to unify schemas and enable metadata-driven query pruning in Snowflake, reducing data processed and improving performance, along with lessons learned operating at scale.
room: Basecamp South Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

title: Powering the Future of Merchant Experiences with Snowflake Cortex AI
description: Learn how Global Payments is integrating a talk-to-your-data agent powered by Snowflake Cortex AI into its merchant portal. This innovation allows merchants to use natural language queries to instantly extract insights from complex business data, eliminating the need for reports or SQL. The customer value is instant, actionable insights for faster decision-making. Merchants can ask direct questions like "sales trend last month?" or "highest dispute status?" and receive reliable answers from both structured and unstructured data. This democratizes data access, accelerates daily operations, and offers a personalized, efficient self-service experience, freeing up merchant time for strategic growth
speaker_title: SVP of Data Engineering and Platforms

title: Security Benefit Achieves 4× Faster Legacy Migration with Snowflake and Coalesce
description: Security Benefit transformed its fragmented data stack by standardizing on Snowflake and Coalesce. Replacing legacy CDP pipelines built across AWS services, Python and SQL Server, the team rebuilt a critical process 75% faster, shrinking a two-month migration to two weeks. Engineers now test transformation approaches in 15-20 minutes instead of over a week, accelerating innovation while strengthening governance and lineage. Join this session to learn how Security Benefit modernized its pipelines, improved security with RBAC and dynamic masking, and built a scalable Snowflake foundation ready for AI.

title: Smaller, Smarter, Cheaper: Custom LLMs Built in Snowflake
description: General-purpose LLMs are powerful but often expensive and not specialized for enterprise use. As AI scales, organizations need models that are cheaper, smaller and deeply aligned to proprietary data. In this session, we show you how to take advantage of comprehensive solutions for LLM customization, across all levels of complexity, directly in Snowflake. From APIs that simplify the workflow and provide easy experimentation loops to research-focused capabilities supporting reinforcement learning and advanced optimization, teams can start simple and evolve seamlessly, while ensuring they meet their quality, cost and latency goals.

title: Snowflake-Hosted AI-Accelerated Analytics Catalog
description: Centene Corporation presents the Enterprise Data Catalog Streamlit App, a solution to centralize metadata and analytics assets from Snowflake, Alation and Power BI to deliver consistent, governed data discovery. Features include searchable assets, AI-generated descriptions, data lineage visualization and a dataset/report review system. The platform's key innovation is a semantic model accessible via a natural language chatbot, enabling users to easily and quickly find the right enterprise dataset or report. This transformation accelerates data discovery, fosters governance, and empowers confident, data-backed decision-making across Centene.

title: What’s New: Optimize, Govern and Scale Your Snowflake Environment
description: Stop managing your Snowflake environment — start architecting it. This session shows how to master complex, multi-account ecosystems at scale without administrative overhead. Explore how Organizations 2.0 enables multi-account visibility and control, and how the Well-Architected Framework drives platform health through automated blueprints and health checks. Discover the latest innovations for precise cost governance and see how Cortex AI transforms administration with intelligent automation to standardize best practices and streamline enterprise operations.
speakers: Zaki Bajwa; Pranav Pokharel; Srujana Kandimalla

title: BI to Generative BI
description: Learn how we built a data-to-AI pipeline using Snowflake Intelligence and Semantic Views, resulting in 10x faster data modeling and instantaneous insights for both our traditional BI dashboards and generative AI agents. We will detail the technical journey, how we solved the challenges of governance using Semantic Views to ground our AI, and the blueprints for building a data-driven organization where every decision is powered by trusted analytics and explainable generative AI.
room: Basecamp South Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

title: Bridging the Gap: How BNC Migrated Legacy PySpark to Snowpark Unified Compute
description: As data volumes scale, the friction between external Apache Spark clusters and cloud warehouses often creates latency and governance challenges. BNC addressed this by migrating PySpark pipelines directly into Snowflake’s unified compute platform using Snowpark Connect. By eliminating external infrastructure management while maintaining Python-based flexibility, BNC streamlined its operations and simplified data oversight. This session explores the technical architecture of the migration, strategies for handling complex dependency management, and benchmark results comparing their legacy Spark environment to the new, optimized Snowflake implementation.
speaker_title: AI Lead at National Bank of Canada
room: Basecamp South Theater 4 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

title: How TripAdvisor Unlocked Its Data to Make Every Traveler Better
description: TripAdvisor set out to make every traveler's experience more personal — but their legacy CDP was holding them back. In this session, learn how TripAdvisor migrated to a composable CDP on Snowflake, centralizing complete customer and event data while giving marketers true self-serve access. Hear the challenges they faced along the way, and the results that followed: AI-powered real-time decisions, lower acquisition costs, a thriving retail media network, and richer, more personalized experiences that make every traveler better.
speaker_title: Sr. Director Data & AI Strategy and Ops

title: Modern Data Governance and Security at Scale with Snowflake at T-Mobile
description: As data, AI and analytics adoption accelerates across T-Mobile’s Snowflake environment — supporting over 4,000 users, 3,100+ virtual warehouses and more than 10 petabytes of data — strong governance and security are essential to enabling trust, scale and innovation. This session explores our security strategy, including the move away from passwords toward stronger authentication mechanisms, continuous monitoring of security posture, network access controls and SOX controls. We will also highlight how T-Mobile enables emerging AI security considerations and governed data sharing across all departments through an internal data marketplace that balances accessibility with enterprise-grade governance.

title: New Opportunities in the Consumer Goods Industry with AI and Accessible Data
description: The consumer goods industry is unlocking new opportunities with AI and technological advancements. This session will explore emerging trends shaping the market and how brands are leveraging data-driven strategies to drive growth, improve efficiency and enhance consumer engagement. Leading experts will provide industry insights, followed by a discussion with a consumer goods executive on how to harness AI and data analytics to transform the company. Join us to discover how leading companies stay agile, competitive and future-ready in a rapidly evolving market.
speakers: Ryan Wolbeck; Freddy Guard Guard; Sam Wong
speaker_title: Head of AI and MLE Platform

title: Nissan's Journey to Connected Product Intelligence and Agent-Driven Innovation
description: Join Nissan's CDO, Francois Peltier, as he shares Nissan's transformation toward an intelligent, connected product ecosystem powered by Snowflake. Discover how Nissan bridges vehicle, customer, and dealer 360 through connected products data, enabling a data mesh architecture that allows real-time decision-making. Learn their approach to building certified data products that unlock new revenue streams through connected mobility, personalized customer experiences and predictive maintenance. They'll showcase breakthrough use cases including Bill of Materials forecasting, digital customer behavior analytics and competitive dealer pricing intelligence through connected product insights.

title: Ontology on Snowflake: From Unified Data to AI-Powered Intelligence
description: Discover how Snowflake’s ontology-driven architecture unifies data, semantics and AI. This session explores how to model real-world knowledge directly on Snowflake using metadata, inference rules and ontology layers — then reason over it with Snowflake Cortex Analyst and Snowflake Intelligence. See how consistent semantics enable context-aware analytics, natural language reasoning and a foundation for truly intelligent data applications — all built natively on Snowflake.
room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

title: The CDO Playbook in the Era of AI: The CDO Executive Panel
description: The CDO organization sits at the center of today’s AI transformation.
