Learn how unified data, strengthened governance and cloud‑native architecture accelerate AI adoption, ensure compliance, and deliver responsible, explainable and enterprise‑grade AI at scale.
room: Basecamp South Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

title: What’s New: Build and Deploy Apps at Lightning Speed
description: Bring your code to your data, and go from ideas to apps in minutes. Snowflake Apps is evolving to bridge the gap between your code and the enterprise data ecosystem. We’ll unveil the launch of native support for modern frontend frameworks like React and showcase new integrations with AI-powered development workflows. Discover how to move from a natural language prompt to a secure, production-ready app in record time, and hear from companies that are already building faster on Snowflake.

title: What's New: Simplify Warehouse Management with Snowflake Compute Capabilities
description: Experience the next generation of the Snowflake engine as it evolves into a single, adaptive compute layer. This session explores how this unified architecture drives price performance, delivering the latest hardware and software innovation to run more queries for the same cost with operational ease and lower TCO. Discover how Interactive tables and warehouses eliminate manual tuning for sub-second, high-concurrency analytics, and how Snowpipe Streaming and CDC simplify production-ready pipelines — all with the ease and zero operational overhead of a fully managed platform.

title: Zero Latency Analytics on Demand: Scaling BI Expertise with Agentic AI
description: Whatnot is the fastest-growing online marketplace ever. It's a live shopping platform where users spend 80 to 90 minutes per day, and sellers host over 20 million minutes of live content every week. Data and BI teams find themselves buried in ad-hoc reporting requests: "Can you check this query?" "What happened with this seller?" Every answer pulled them away from strategic partnership with business leaders. Whatnot will show how the team escaped this "help desk" trap by building an agentic Slackbot powered by Snowflake Cortex Analyst and Semantic Views. The company reduced time-to-answer to sub-minute latency and cut analyst distractions to a minimum, replacing manual triage with governed AI.

title: AI and the New Software Lifecycle: From Code to Production
description: AI is changing how software is built and operated, from generating and reviewing code to testing, deployment and reliability. But the impact is uneven and introduces new risks. This panel brings CTOs together to examine how the software lifecycle is actually evolving. Where does AI meaningfully accelerate delivery and where does it create new bottlenecks? How do CI/CD, debugging and incident response change when AI generates code? Expect candid perspectives on what’s working, where it breaks down and how engineering leaders should adapt.
room: The Alpine Clubhouse Executive Theater (Moscone South, Level 3)

title: AI TECHNIQUES THAT BOOST SOCIAL, MARKETING AND LIVE EVENT PERFORMANCE
description: Media and entertainment, marketing and social teams need deeper insight into how audiences engage with video. Snowflake Cortex AI Video Processing enables hook-strength prediction, live-event retention analysis and ad-break optimization, helping organizations reduce drop-off, improve message impact and make smarter content decisions directly in the Snowflake AI Data Cloud.
speakers: Jessie E Felix Felix; James Cha-Earley

title: BEYOND THE SILO: AI-DRIVEN MANUFACTURING WITH SNOWFLAKE, SAP AND AWS
description: Manufacturing leaders are trapped in data silos, unable to connect SAP's business context with the speed AI-powered operations demand. This session shows how leading manufacturers are unifying SAP S/4HANA, Snowflake and AWS into an AI-ready data foundation. Walk through the architecture for zero-copy data sharing and multi-source harmonization, eliminating ETL pipelines while preserving SAP's business semantics. See how static ERP records become dynamic, closed-loop outcomes: real-time scenario modeling, automated SAP write-backs and AI-optimized make-to-order workflows. Leave with a practical blueprint that turns enterprise data into a competitive advantage.

title: BUILDING REAL-TIME FRAUD DETECTION WITH AGENTIC ML IN SNOWFLAKE
description: Moving ML from experimentation to production shouldn't require rebuilding from scratch or navigating lengthy development cycles. In this hands-on session, you'll build an end-to-end fraud detection model using an agentic approach in Snowflake. Starting with natural language prompts in Cortex Code from Snowflake Notebooks, you'll train a model, register it to the Snowflake Model Registry and deploy it for real-time inference, all without leaving Snowflake. Walk away with practical techniques for creating production-ready prediction endpoints and scaling online inference workloads for fraud detection and beyond.

title: DITCH DIY, BUILD WITH AI: LEVERAGE AGENTS AND SDK FOR CUSTOM CONNECTORS
description: Building custom data connectors often requires weeks of engineering effort, ongoing maintenance and complex API integrations. In this demo, see how Fivetran’s AI Connector Agent and Connector SDK use AI to generate production-ready connectors for REST API-based sources — automatically handling authentication, pagination and schema mapping — and deliver data into Snowflake in minutes. Learn how data teams can quickly connect unsupported or custom sources without writing integration code or managing fragile pipelines. Discover how Fivetran's AI Connector Agent provides a faster, more reliable alternative to DIY builds, reducing engineering overhead so teams can focus on unlocking value from data.
speaker_title: Sr. Director Global Partner Sales Eng

title: FROM TANKERS TO TICKERS: SPOT MARKET STRESS EARLIER WITH CONNECTED DATA
description: Geopolitical disruption does not stay contained. It ripples through trade routes, commodity flows, supplier networks and market expectations, often creating signals before the full impact is obvious. In this session, we show how connected S&P Global data on Snowflake, queryable with AI, helps teams trace those effects from the physical world to financial markets more quickly. See how connected shipping and cargo data can reveal stress building across critical trade routes, and how supplier momentum can help anticipate downstream pressure on companies and markets. We also demo how Snowflake enables efficient processing of large supply chain data sets at scale.
speaker_title: Global Head of New Product Development

title: HANDS-ON LAB: AUTONOMOUS SQL PIPELINES FOR AI AGENTS
description: Modern data engineering is no longer just about moving data or building static data pipelines; it’s about making data AI ready at scale. In this hands-on workshop, we'll build an end-to-end data pipeline to power an AI agent in Snowflake. We'll use Snowflake Openflow to ingest REST API data and semi-structured documents into Snowflake. We'll define our pipeline objects declaratively, implement Dynamic Tables to quickly transform raw data into structured assets, and use Cortex Code along to way to generate new data models and objects. Finally, we'll build a Cortex Agents and use it with Snowflake Intelligence to extract insights from the data via a conversational interface.

title: SCALE AI CAPABILITIES WITH GENPACT’S AUTONOMOUS AI PLATFORM ON SNOWFLAKE
description: Explore how Genpact’s Snowflake native autonomous AI platform, powered by Snowflake Cortex AI, supports enterprise-scale AI enablement. Built on AI Maestro™, Genpact’s advanced platform for managing AI workflows, and delivered through the Genpact AI Gigafactory model, this solution enhances the delivery and deployment of AI systems. It supports AI CoE setup with governed ecosystems designed to foster enterprise-wide AI adoption and deliver measurable business value. Learn actionable strategies to scale AI, enhance intelligent enterprise operations and achieve meaningful business outcomes. Explore this opportunity to stay at the forefront of AI innovation.

title: THE BUILDER’S GUIDE TO SIGMA AI APPS: WORKFLOWS, CORTEX AGENTS AND AI FUNCTIONS
description: What separates an AI app from a chatbot? Action tools that write data, control the UI and send notifications. This lab teaches data professionals to build code-free agentic workflows.

title: AI APPLIED MASTERY: SKILL-BASED AI ENGINEERING CURRICULUM IN THE AGENTIC WORLD
description: From knowledge to measurable skills and impact, this session explores how Snowflake Academia and Northeastern University are redefining AI education for the agentic era. We start with imparting AI knowledge and then move on to project-driven mastery, where students solve realistic problems by building knowledge graphs and agentic AI and data engineering workflows on Snowflake’s free-to-academic AI platform. Enhanced by industry guest lecture, mentorship and iterative innovation, this model bridges the gap between classroom and career. Learn how to empower students to architect real-world solutions using contextual engineering and the latest AI skills. The future is skills based; let’s build it.

title: AUTONOMOUS DATA PIPELINES FOR EXPLAINABLE AI: FROM INSIGHT TO SOURCE
description: When AI-driven decisions are made, you must prove the data's source. AI analytics tools like Snowflake Intelligence operate on the right side of the data stack (BI/warehouse data), but trust is built on the left side, where operational data moves through pipelines. As AI scales, schema drift and evolving logic make explaining metrics difficult. Maia closes this gap by managing the data system feeding Snowflake. It builds, executes and adapts pipelines, capturing context and lineage by default, ensuring an end-to-end view from insight to source.

title: BUILT FOR HUMANS, READY FOR AGENTS: A DATA STACK THAT SERVES EVERYONE
description: The next wave of self-serve analytics won't be driven by better dashboards. It'll be driven by agents that find, interpret and act on data autonomously. But most data stacks weren't built for this. In this session, you'll learn how agentic workloads differ from analytical ones and what that means for your tooling, models, freshness SLAs and access patterns. We'll cover the decisions that create optionality versus lock-in — storage formats, compute separation, semantic layers and metadata richness — and see how open source changes the calculus. Drawing on real trade-offs from Airbyte, you'll leave with a practical framework to assess your own stack.

title: CONSISTENCY BEFORE INTELLIGENCE: THE HIDDEN WORK BEHIND AI ANALYTICS
description: We’re at a moment in the industry where every data professional is trying to reach the AI-powered future of analytics: where reliable insights are generated instantly on demand, freeing our analysts from ad hoc data requests to pursue more strategic work. However, many of us have found that simply plugging an AI to our data warehouse won’t magically get us there. At Canva, we’ve identified three big things that need to happen first: an AI-ready data warehouse architecture, a semantic layer to assist and govern our LLM assistants, and a solution to metric consistency. In this session, we dive into each of those three major aspects of our journey toward the future of analytics.
speakers: Nick Preema; Vincey Au; Sida Yuan

title: DRIVING EFFICIENCY AND COST SAVINGS WITH SNOWFLAKE NATIVE TO ICEBERG
description: Enterprises often run Snowflake native tables alongside open formats, driving duplicate storage, complex governance and higher costs. Capital One partnered with Slalom and Snowflake to migrate Collaboration and Sandbox workloads from Snowflake native to Iceberg tables in OneLake (S3). This session covers the target architecture, migration and automation patterns, and how defaulting table creation to Iceberg simplified adoption. Attendees will learn how this approach reduced vendor lock-in, eliminated redundant storage and delivered measurable cost savings at scale.

title: FROM DATA TO DIALOGUE: MAKING AI USEFUL IN THE BUSINESS
description: As AI capabilities become native within Snowflake, the challenge shifts from experimentation to real-world application. In this session, Under Armour shares how it is enabling conversational access to data across sales, product and consumer domains. Learn how users interact with both structured and unstructured data through natural language powered by Snowflake Cortex AI. The session focuses on the patterns, data foundations and governance approaches supporting these solutions today, along with practical considerations for teams looking to apply Snowflake’s AI capabilities to real business use cases.

title: PREP EXPOSURE‑LEVEL ADVERTISING DATA FOR ENTERPRISE AI WITH SNOWFLAKE CORTEX
description: Exposure‑level advertising data is one of the most powerful — and most difficult — assets to operationalize analytics and AI. In this session, The Trade Desk shows how it transforms trillions of granular exposure logs into a governed, AI‑ready data product in Snowflake that can be used by clients, analytics teams and Snowflake Intelligence.

title: THE FUTURE OF MANUFACTURING: ENABLING DATA AGENTS WITH IT, OT AND IOT DATA
description: The manufacturing industry has reached an inflection point, when AI agents can reason across plant-floor telemetry, maintenance logs and ERP systems to predict equipment failures and manage supply chain disruptions. Yet, how do organizations build a data foundation that enables agents to operate securely and autonomously across the enterprise? Join the manufacturing keynote at Snowflake Summit to learn more about the practical implementation of agentic workflows. Hear from industry experts and pioneering manufacturers, moving beyond pilot projects to deploy production agents that drive business results. This event outlines the strategic roadmap to prepare for the agentic future in manufacturing.
speakers: Francois Peltier; Tim Long; Tony Leopold

title: THE FUTURE OF MARKETING: TURNING BIG MOMENTS INTO MEASURABLE ROI
description: Super Bowl. Olympics. Global stages where millions are invested — and impact is notoriously hard to prove. In this session, hear how Viral Nation uses Snowflake to measure real-time Super Bowl performance through social sentiment and “viral velocity,” and how Snowflake’s own marketing team quantifies the long-term brand impact of its Olympic partnership. Discover how AI in the Snowflake AI Data Cloud turns high-stakes moments into measurable outcomes — transforming prestige, attention and sponsorships into defensible ROI.
speakers: Richelle Batuigas; Erin Foxworthy Foxworthy; Christine Okubo

title: THE FUTURE OF PUBLIC SECTOR: SCALING AI FOR MISSION IMPACT
description: The AI mandate is now a defining priority for the public sector. The technology is reshaping how agencies approach their most critical priorities — from enhancing citizen services to improving security postures to increasing government efficiencies. But implementing AI requires a unified data foundation, clear strategy and alignment to measurable outcomes. Join the public sector keynote to hear how leaders are modernizing legacy systems, unifying data and embedding AI into critical workflows. We'll highlight the latest Snowflake innovations, showcase real customer success stories and outline a blueprint for how organizations can leverage AI today.

title: WHAT'S NEW: SWIFTLY TAKE YOUR IDEAS TO PRODUCTION WITH CORTEX CODE
description: Transform your ideas into production-ready workflows faster by using the AI coding agent designed specifically for data to handle everything from admin, data engineering, machine learning and agent-building tasks. Learn how Cortex Code can simplify and automate your toughest tasks and help your team expedite time-to-value.

title: YOUR DATA PLATFORM IS NOW YOUR APP PLATFORM: 300% ROI WITH SNOWFLAKE, SIGMA, DBT
description: Building customer applications used to require buying and maintaining an entire application stack — cost, complexity and technical debt included. Lumelight, a national employee benefits firm, skipped all of that. Working with Coastal, Lumelight used Snowflake, Sigma and dbt to turn its own claims and reporting data into two revenue-generating applications, delivering 300% ROI at 60%-70% margins. In this session, see how they built it and how you can turn your own data into a product your customers will pay for.

title: BUILD BETTER SEMANTIC VIEWS USING CORTEX CODE AND GLEAN
description: This session shows how Glean, the Work AI platform, connects to Cortex Code via MCP to bring enterprise context directly into Snowflake builders’ dev workflows. We cover the value of Glean and Snowflake for conversational analytics, then demo Glean’s integration with Cortex Code using a finance schema spanning subscriptions, customers, invoices and MRR snapshots. We’ll show how a semantic view optimizer in Cortex Code inspects DDL, searches finance SOPs and Slack via Glean, refines metrics such as active customers and churn, and then compares the original and optimized views in Snowflake Cortex Analyst.
speaker_title: Head of Partner Solutions, ISVs Glean

title: BUILDING AN INTEROPERABLE LAKEHOUSE WITH SNOWFLAKE HORIZON AND CORTEX CODE
description: Multiple engines and catalogs make governance hard and AI‑ready data even harder. In this session, we will show how to use Snowflake Horizon and Cortex Code to build an interoperable lakehouse that is secure, well-governed and easy to extend. We will start by designing Iceberg-based lakehouse, then use Horizon to centralize discovery and policy enforcement across engines. On top of this governed foundation, we will introduce Cortex Code as the developer copilot that generates and refactors the data lake objects. Attendees will leave with patterns and code templates to operationalize an interoperable data lake on Snowflake.

title: BUILDING CONTEXT-AWARE AGENTS WITH SNOWFLAKE AND GOOGLE CLOUD VERTEX AI
description: In the race to deploy generative AI, the biggest hurdle isn't the model — it’s the context. Accessing data in the Snowflake AI Data Cloud is often blocked by security silos and complex pipelines, but the seamless integration between Snowflake and Google Cloud eliminates this barrier. Join our session to explore the synergy of Snowflake’s governed data and Google’s Gemini models on Vertex AI. We will use our Quickstart integration to demonstrate secure data flows for context-aware AI agents capable of nuanced reasoning over your enterprise data. This session delivers a proven blueprint to advance from AI experimentation to production-grade intelligence, without the architectural overhead.

title: DAAS ON SNOWFLAKE: DESIGNING FOR SCALE, GOVERNANCE AND DECISION INTELLIGENCE
description: As analytics adoption within Comcast Supply Chain scaled from hundreds to more than 1,000 users, traditional data products revealed limitations in reuse, consistency and governance. We implemented data as a service (DaaS) on Snowflake, enabling governed, scalable data consumption across a diverse user base. We walk through the architecture, operating model and lessons learned, showing how DaaS provides a resilient foundation for intelligent decision workflows and future agent-based capabilities — while maintaining flexibility, trust and strong governance.

title: GOLDEN RECORDS: AI-READY DATA PRODUCTS WITH SNOWFLAKE NATIVE APPS
description: Master data management (MDM) is the missing link between Medallion lakehouses and reliable AI/BI outcomes. In this session, you’ll see how Semarchy runs as a Snowflake Native App to unify trusted golden records directly in the Snowflake AI Data Cloud — with no egress costs and no extra infrastructure. We’ll walk through an end-to-end certification design pattern to: Ingest and match, choose field survivorship, workflow-driven stewardship and publishing of governed data products. We’ll also cover hybrid patterns that support both analytical and transactional needs using Snowflake Hybrid Tables and Snowflake Postgres.

title: HOW AMPERITY HELPED THE SEAHAWKS BUILD A UNIFIED FAN IDENTITY IN THREE MINUTES
description: The Seattle Seahawks couldn't identify their highest-value fans. Fan identity was limited to email addresses, ticket data was siloed to single-game purchases, and there was no way to connect across departments. By building a unified fan data ecosystem on Amperity, the Seahawks replaced fragmented profiles with rich fan views, including lifetime value scores, top-fan segmentation and passholder flags. Snowflake Bridge cut data sharing to three minutes with automatic daily updates. The outcome: 5,000 previously unidentifiable fans, now fully visible and reachable. Hear what this means for your own fan and customer data strategy.

title: THE CEO’S AI MANDATE: THE CHIEF EXECUTIVE PANEL
description: We are in a period of seismic change that is fundamentally redefining the nature of work and enterprise leadership. For the CEO, AI is no longer a technical roadmap item; it is a strategic reset of the entire business engine. This cross-vertical panel of chief executives explores the transition from defensive risk management to high-velocity value creation. Key areas of focus are how organizations can scale AI adoption, lean in on transformation, build a more enabled workforce and capture business value.

title: UNDER THE HOOD OF SNOWFLAKE HYBRID TABLES
description: Peek under the hood to learn how hybrid table architecture delivers mission-critical performance and reliability for your applications while reducing build time and complexity through full native Snowflake database integration. We also dive into recent optimizations that have delivered a 10x improvement in transactional workload performance.

title: USING THE ORG ACCOUNT TO PROTECT DATA PRODUCTS IN THE INTERNAL MARKETPLACE
description: Sharing data internally doesn’t compromise on security or speed. This session showcases a hands-on approach to protecting sensitive data products using Snowflake Organization User Groups. We’ll show how centralized governance via the organization account can be pushed down to local accounts seamlessly and how to use this to protect your sensitive data. Learn how to architect row-access and masking policies that reference org-level groups, ensuring consistent protection across your entire data mesh to automate data privacy and scale your internal marketplace with confidence.

title: WHAT'S NEW: SIMPLIFY WAREHOUSE MANAGEMENT WITH SNOWFLAKE COMPUTE CAPABILITIES
description: Experience the next generation of the Snowflake engine as it evolves into a single, adaptive compute layer. This session explores how this unified architecture drives price performance, delivering the latest hardware and software innovation to run more queries for the same cost with operational ease and lower TCO. Discover how Interactive tables and warehouses eliminate manual tuning for sub-second, high-concurrency analytics, and how Snowpipe Streaming and CDC simplify production-ready pipelines — all with the ease and zero operational overhead of a fully managed platform.

title: WHAT'S NEW: UNIFY YOUR LAKEHOUSE WITH SNOWFLAKE SIMPLE INTEROPERABILITY
description: Pipeline sprawl and unnecessary data movement are the hidden taxes to your AI innovation. Discover how Snowflake’s platform empowers you to work from a single governed copy of data, wherever it lives. Built on native support for Apache Polaris™ and Apache Iceberg™, this session explores how to power new use cases with Iceberg V3 support and virtually eliminate storage complexity with Snowflake Storage, all without vendor lock-in. We'll also cover strategies to reduce governance and security risks by building a metadata-driven universal governance model to fuel human and agentic intelligence with access to governed, live data.

title: BEST PRACTICES FOR ENTERPRISE-READY MLOPS WITH SNOWFLAKE
description: In this session we'll dive deep on best practices for managing enterprise-ready machine learning pipelines at scale in Snowflake. We'll cover the orchestration of training and inference workflows, model lifecycle management, administrative best practices, RBAC models, CI/CD pipelines and more. January Technologies will share how it used these tools to take its machine learning projects from experimentation to deployment.

title: DELIVER RELIABLE, EXPLAINABLE, TRACEABLE AGENTS WITH DBT
description: Agents can generate outputs in seconds, but without visibility into their reasoning, data sources and logic, teams can't trust them in production. This session shows how dbt powers trusted Cortex Agents through a governed context layer and high-precision retrieval — ensuring agents start from the right models and metrics, cite their sources, align to approved definitions and fail safely when context is unavailable. Using dbt agent workflows, you can deploy autonomous agents with traceable, reviewable outputs. Leave with a practical framework for moving from agent demos to production-ready deployments you can trust.

title: END‑TO‑END DATA MANAGEMENT FOR SNOWFLAKE: FROM DESIGN TO DELIVERED VALUE
description: Most organizations don’t struggle with data — they struggle with the upstream work required to make data usable. This session shows how to close the gaps between design-before-ingest, modeling, pipeline construction, governance and productization so what enters Snowflake is already clean, structured and aligned to business meaning. You’ll learn how to streamline the flow from upstream definition to downstream consumption, automate delivery of governed data products, and gain visibility into performance, cost and usage so Snowflake runs as cleanly as the pipelines feeding it.

title: FROM LEGACY TO AI-READY: PUBLIC SECTOR DATA MODERNIZATION PANEL
description: In the era of AI, modernizing data is now a mission-critical imperative. While moving off legacy systems is challenging, it is a necessity to improve efficiency, reduce costs and accelerate mission outcomes. In this session, public sector leaders share how they are building a cloud-native, organizational data strategy with a secure and governed foundation. Discover how these modern architectures enable better decision-making and cross-agency collaboration. We will move beyond the "why" to the "how," providing a roadmap to transform technical debt into a scalable runway for future generative AI and agentic initiatives.

title: HOW NOKIA TURNS TELECOM DATA INTO SHAREABLE ANALYTICAL PRODUCTS ON SNOWFLAKE
description: Discover how Nokia transforms siloed telecom data — CDRs, network telemetry, and subscriber KPIs — into governed, shareable analytical data products on Snowflake. Learn how they use Secure Data Sharing and Native Apps to deliver real-time insights directly into operator environments without data movement and enable CSPs to query complex telecom data through shared semantic models. See how Nokia's architecture turns static network data sets into interactive, AI-enriched tools that service providers can operationalize immediately.

title: HOW TRUIST IS BUILDING THE DATA FOUNDATION THAT MAKES CORTEX AI POSSIBLE
description: In a regulated bank, AI is only as reliable as the data feeding it. Truist is building that foundation on Snowflake with Ataccama, starting with FDIC regulatory reporting, and embedding a data trust layer into the Snowflake medallion architecture. Now they're expanding further, migrating more of their data estate into Snowflake, rolling the architecture across additional regulatory programs, and building toward the point where Cortex AI runs on data they already trust. Their team shares what they built, where they're taking it and what any regulated institution needs to prioritize to get there.

title: SCALING AI IN MANUFACTURING: EXECUTIVE PANEL
description: The AI conversation in manufacturing has shifted from "if" to "how fast." This executive panel explores how industry giants leverage Snowflake to move beyond experimental pilots into scalable, production-ready AI. Core discussion pillars:
speakers: Tim Long; Vishwa Ram; Thierry Martin

title: SMARTER MODERNIZATION: HOW SNOWCONVERT AI IS REDEFINING MIGRATION TO SNOWFLAKE
description: Over the past year, SnowConvert AI has transformed from a code translation tool into a fully intelligent modernization engine. In this session, the SnowConvert AI team reveals what’s new: deeper AI integration, support for partial and incremental migrations, smarter schema mapping, and a completely reimagined user experience designed for speed and transparency. Learn how these innovations make migrations to Snowflake faster, more flexible and more predictable than ever before — helping organizations modernize with confidence and unlock the full power of the Snowflake Data Cloud.

title: TRUSTED AGENTS: DELIVERING RELIABLE DATA AND AI SYSTEMS AT SCALE
description: In 2026, data and AI leaders will be measured by the success of AI initiatives. So how can teams move beyond failing pilots and toward reliable, production-grade AI at scale? In this session, Barr Moses, CEO and Co-Founder of Monte Carlo, will explore how enterprise teams should monitor the most critical components of agents — inputs, operational metrics and outputs — to ensure they’re trusted and well adopted. Attendees will learn best practices for operationalizing data and AI observability and leveraging Snowflake as a critical nexus to close the loop between data inputs and AI outputs.

title: WHAT'S NEW: AGENTIC ML IN SNOWFLAKE
description: Learn how the latest agentic capabilities in Snowflake ML accelerate productivity for your ML teams. Join us for new feature overviews and a live demo showcasing our latest innovations.

title: WHAT’S NEW: ZERO TO A TRUSTED SEMANTIC LAYER IN SNOWFLAKE
description: Stop struggling with fragmented logic and start building a trusted data foundation in minutes. Join us to see how easy it is to create Snowflake Semantic Views that turn raw data into governed, actionable insights. We’ll demonstrate how our connected ecosystem allows your business logic to flow seamlessly from your data cloud to your AI agents and BI tools. This session also features a live demo of our latest "easy-to-build" semantic innovations.

title: $14T+ ASSET MANAGER'S DATA TRANSFORMATION: BLACKROCK'S GOLDEN SOURCE STRATEGY
description: Data is the lifeblood of modern finance. For BlackRock, which manages over $14 trillion in assets, high-fidelity data is the foundation of its Aladdin platform. To centralize and distribute massive third-party data sets, BlackRock has engineered the Aladdin Data Factory to govern thousands of complex incoming feeds. By leveraging Snowflake’s data-sharing capabilities, BlackRock maintains an elite ecosystem of automated pipelines, metadata management and granular entitlements, delivering "intelligence as a service" to both internal teams and global clients.

title: 20-MINUTE THREAT HUNT: BUILDING AND DEPLOYING A CYBERSECURITY AGENT WITH CORTEX
description: Stop building custom data pipelines for AI. Start solving threats in minutes. This session covers the end-to-end process to rapidly deploy a production-ready, investigative generative AI agent for cybersecurity using Snowflake Intelligence:

title: BEYOND CHAT: ACTIVATING SNOWFLAKE CORTEX AGENTS FOR ENTERPRISE PRODUCTIVITY
description: Is your AI stuck in a chat box? To drive real ROI, Snowflake Cortex Agents must be governed and integrated into your broader business processes. This session introduces the Boomi Agent Control Tower, the central hub for managing agents from Snowflake, Bedrock and beyond. Learn how to fuel agents with real-time data and business context for maximum accuracy; move from "passive assistants" to "active workflows" that trigger tasks in Salesforce, SAP and others; and sync Cortex Agents into a multiprovider governance plane for agent trust. Ready to move from isolated bots to a high-performing, governed agentic workforce that turns Snowflake data into enterprise impact? Join us.
speaker_title: SVP Solution Engineering and Data Management GTM

title: BEYOND CUSTOMER 360: EXTENDING IDENTITY FOR CLEAN ROOM COLLABORATION
description: Join us in exploring this unprecedented session on how Kargo leverages Snowflake Data Clean Rooms and TransUnion’s identity resolution to deliver high-quality, privacy-centric ad segments for publishers and retail media networks. By bringing together publisher IP/MAID data and TransUnion identity with marketing attributes inside Snowflake’s multiparty clean room, Kargo runs more secure, policy-enforced three-way joins that minimize the risk of PII exposure. Attendees will learn how multiparty clean rooms with data access policies and identity enrichment drive improved match rates and benefit from minimal data movement and more efficient ad targeting across the ecosystem.

title: CORTEX CODE FOR FULL LIFECYCLE DEVELOPMENT
description: Cortex Code is superb for "help me build a Snowflake object."

title: HONDA’S DATA MESH STRATEGY: APACHE ICEBERG, CORTEX AI AND HORIZON CATALOG
description: As the mobility industry undergoes a once-in-a-century transformation, Honda drives rapid software-defined vehicle innovation by redefining its data platform. To match this pace, we established a foundational strategy that balances agility with governance. This session unveils our hybrid data architecture, seamlessly integrating centralized governance with a flexible Data Mesh. Learn how we harness Snowflake to build an AI-ready data mesh, utilizing innovations like Snowflake Horizon, Iceberg Tables and Cortex AI capabilities to create a vehicle platform that continuously evolves.

title: HOW GITLAB SAVED 90% IN ENGINEERING COSTS BY KEEPING AI INSIDE SNOWFLAKE
description: GitLab had more than 75,000 sensitive Zendesk tickets full of valuable feedback that were inaccessible due to governance constraints, and manual review wasn’t scalable. An external AI pipeline seemed viable until we uncovered its real cost: complex setup, ongoing maintenance and over $73,000 a year in operational burden. Instead, we chose the simpler path: Snowflake Cortex AI. By keeping all processing inside Snowflake’s governance boundary, we eliminated infrastructure overhead and processed all 75,000+ tickets for about $1,500 without any data leaving our account.

title: NEO4J AND SNOWFLAKE: TURN FLAT TABLES INTO GRAPH INTELLIGENCE
description: Learn how Neo4j unlocks graph insights from relational Snowflake tables. Use Graph Analytics for Snowflake to run graph algorithms directly on Snowflake data — no ETL required. With Snowflake Cortex AI integration, you can describe a business problem in plain language and generate graph-powered insights written back to a Snowflake table. Graph analytics help solve problems like fraud detection, customer 360, recommendations and supply chain optimization by revealing relationships hidden in tabular data. When a full property graph is the best fit, Neo4j’s Data Importer makes it to bring Snowflake data into Neo4j with gen AI-assisted graph model generation and simple point-and-click schema editing.

title: SNOW IN SAN DIEGO: OPERATIONAL EFFICIENCY AND PASSENGER SAFETY AT SAN
description: San Diego International Airport (SAN) is partnering with Snowflake to architect the next generation of Fleet Intelligence, turning high-volume telemetry from aircraft and ground equipment into actionable foresight. Beyond monitoring the present, SAN applies time series and geospatial analytics to fuse real-time data with forward-looking flight operations. For this busy, single-runway airport, the shift is from reactive tracking to predictive resource orchestration. Learn how SAN uses this pilot to surface bottlenecks before they occur, optimize asset allocation and keep passenger flow smooth — transforming raw, third-party enriched data into operational insight in minutes at scale.
speaker_company: San Diego Co Regional Airport Authority

title: THE FRONTIER OF AGENTIC ENTERPRISE: FOUNDERS, VCS AND SNOWFLAKE
description: AI agents are moving from demo to deployment — and the implications for enterprise leaders are massive. Hear directly from Snowflake, Matan Grinbert (CEO & Co-Founder at Factory.ai), João Moura (CEO & Founder at CrewAI), Madison Faulkner (Partner at NEA Ventures) and Bala Kasiviswanathan (VP, Product Management at Snowflake) on how they are enabling the era of the agentic enterprise through AI agents. Moderated by Jeremiah Owyang (GP at Blitzscaling Ventures), this panel explores what it means to bring agentic AI to the agentic enterprise, why trusted data is the foundation agents need, and how forward-thinking enterprises are already putting agents to work.
speakers: Matan Grinberg; João Moura; Madison Faulkner

title: UNITING SAP AND SNOWFLAKE FOR A ZERO-COPY AND AI-DRIVEN ENTERPRISE
description: Join us alongside a customer as they reveal how the new integration between SAP Business Data Cloud and Snowflake is redefining what’s possible for business innovation and AI. Discover how organizations are seamlessly connecting SAP and Snowflake with zero-copy bidirectional data sharing, unlocking governed and real-time data for every workload, from analytics to enterprise AI. Get a first look at how bringing governed, semantically rich SAP data together with Snowflake is empowering teams to break down silos, accelerate decisions and fuel the next generation of intelligent business, all through the power of an open and collaborative data ecosystem.

title: VIDEOAMP’S UNIFIED MLOPS ARCHITECTURE ON SNOWFLAKE
description: For many enterprises, the barrier to ML value isn't a lack of data; it’s the "operational tax" of fragmented environments. VideoAmp has overcome this by consolidating the entire ML lifecycle — from feature engineering to real-time inference — within the Snowflake AI Data Cloud. This session provides a technical deep dive into VideoAmp’s environment design, focusing on how it eliminated egress costs and cross-platform silos to build a production-ready home for diverse ML use cases at scale.

title: WHAT’S NEW: INNOVATE AT SCALE: POWERING A SECURE AND RESILIENT AI ESTATE
description: This session unveils Snowflake innovations designed to protect sensitive AI assets and ensure continuity. Explore what’s new in security for AI, featuring capabilities to secure Cortex flows, Snowflake Intelligence and Cortex Code. We’ll cover AI security posture in the Trust Center, adaptive guardrails against prompt injection and sandboxing to mitigate local risks. Learn about new data movement policies to prevent exfiltration and seamless agent access via agentic identity and JIT provisioning as well as Account Replication 2.0, the next evolution of our BCDR engine. Join us to see these features in action and build a bulletproof, AI-ready estate.
speakers: Radhika Janardanan; Nitika Gupta; Sid Ramadoss

title: AUTOMATING PIPELINES AND WITH SNOWFLAKE OPENFLOW AND CORTEX CODE
description: bpx Energy is an Upstream Oil and Gas company that has used Snowflake Openflow to reduce the costs of their overall tech stack. They started by migrating current pipelines from various data engineering tools to Snowflake Openflow. Once comfortable with the tool, they were excited to be early adopters of Snowflake Openflow with Cortex Code. By using Cortex Code, they built pipelines quickly and migrated off other providers much faster than planned. Snowflake Openflow + Cortex Code saved the bpx team time and money compared to their current stack, and they are already fielding additional requests from the business to use Snowflake Openflow.

title: DATA DISCOVERY AND SEMANTIC CONTEXT FOR AGENTIC AI WITH HORIZON CATALOG
description: Agentic AI can turn data into action, but only if it can find the right data and understand it. That's a challenge since most enterprise data isn’t sufficiently self-describing. In this session, we’ll define what AI-ready data looks like and show how Horizon Catalog provides the critical context layer that helps AI know your data. Horizon Catalog delivers quality-aware context directly to AI agents — so they don’t just access data, they understand it. It also catalogs data from external systems, captures end-to-end lineage and usage patterns, and automates documentation and semantic modeling. Join us to see how a strong metadata foundation enables AI that’s more accurate and trustworthy.

title: DBT FUSION ENGINE: WHAT'S NEW AND HOW TO UPGRADE ON SNOWFLAKE
description: The dbt Fusion engine is here, and if you're running dbt on Snowflake, this is the upgrade you've been waiting for. With native Snowflake ADBC/Arrow integration, Fusion delivers faster query previews, snappier CI runs, and IDE-quality autocomplete and inline errors, all while generating rich semantic metadata that makes your dbt project understandable to both humans and LLMs. In this session, we'll walk through enabling Fusion in dbt on Snowflake and show you exactly what changes in your development experience — including the gotchas — to know before you flip the switch in production.

title: FROM LEGACY TO LIFT-OFF: FIFTH THIRD & TRANSUNION REIMAGINE DATA FOR THE AI ERA
description: Imagine a world where your team isn’t held back by legacy systems or fragmented data. You operate with complete control, and clean, connected, AI-ready data flows instantly where needed. Models refresh and confidently improve. Decisions that took weeks now take minutes. Data teams no longer maintain the past — they build the future. The best part? It’s not a far-fetched dream. Hear how Fifth Third and TransUnion are reimagining data architectures, moving from legacy to cloud-powered foundations, and how Fifth Third leverages TransUnion’s TruIQ Data Enrichment to take more control over how critical data is accessed and linked and to power future-ready analytic and marketing processes.

title: INSIDE SNOWFLAKE: HOW TO MONITOR, ALERT AND STAY AHEAD
description: Have you ever wondered what’s really happening inside Snowflake and the workloads running on it? How do you move beyond treating the platform as a black box, proactively monitor what you run and stay informed as things change? This talk explores how Snowflake monitoring addresses these questions, including AI-driven alerting workflows that enable no-code alerts and notifications. Built on next-generation event tables with improved telemetry management and access, these capabilities help organizations monitor the platform more effectively, with a customer example showing how they are applied in practice.

title: MAKING MAINFRAME DATA AI READY: HYDRATING SNOWFLAKE DATA LAKES
description: Many financial service enterprises still rely on mainframe systems to power critical financial and operational workloads. But activating that data for modern analytics and AI readiness often requires complex pipelines, replication or disruptive modernization.

title: MORGAN STANLEY’S JOURNEY TO AN AI-DRIVEN MARKETING ENGINE
description: For years, Morgan Stanley Wealth Management's use of Snowflake was focused on analytics and reporting. Following a three-year martech modernization, the team transformed this platform into a foundation for omni-channel activation across its brands: E*TRADE, Morgan Stanley Online and MS@Work. With deep integration into Adobe and other martech platforms, leveraging predictive and generative AI models, the platform now provides data, marketing-accelerated campaign execution, improved targeting precision and increased revenue. Hear how the journey unfolded — and what’s next in the year ahead.

title: NTT DOCOMO’S DATA PLATFORM EVOLUTION FOR THE AGENTIC AI ERA
description: In the era of Agentic AI, platforms must truly understand context. Last year, NTT DOCOMO presented an architecture for autonomous network operations. This system is built on scalable Snowflake Openflow pipelines, Apache Iceberg and Cortex AI. This year, we'll share how Snowflake Intelligence and Cortex Agents bridge the semantic gap, allowing AI to analyze perabytes of mobile network data alongside unstructured files like rack diagrams and procurement documents. We'll also show how we moved from centralized storage to a decentralized data mesh, managed through an Internal Marketplace on Snowflake. Join us to see how this approach connects data and gives useful insights to many different users.

title: SCALING AI IN THE PUBLIC SECTOR SHOWCASE: FROM PILOTS TO REAL-WORLD IMPACT
description: Moving from AI "potential" to public sector reality requires more than just code — it requires a roadmap for scale. Join us for a showcase featuring public sector leaders who have successfully transitioned AI from proof of concepts to enterprise-grade production. We’ll dive into key mission outcomes, outline a blueprint for architecture patterns and governance, and share key considerations for how other organizations should navigate the complexities of government environments. We will also outline how Snowflake’s end-to-end AI value proposition — one founded on flexibility, security and ease of use — meet the specific needs of governments globally.

title: THE EVOLVING SAAS DATA PLATFORM: HOW AI AGENTS ARE CONSOLIDATING INTELLIGENCE
description: As enterprises accelerate AI adoption, the modern data stack is shifting from fragmented point tools to intelligence-driven platforms. AI agents are consolidating workflows that once required separate solutions for observability, profiling, lineage and governance — enabling faster decisions and automated remediation. In this session, we explore how Snowflake’s AI-native platform accelerates this shift and how Acceldata’s agentic data management (ADM) provides the governed automation layer that unifies trust, observability and action on top of Snowflake — scaling reliable, decision-ready data operation.

title: WHAT’S NEW: OPTIMIZE, GOVERN AND SCALE YOUR SNOWFLAKE ENVIRONMENT
description: Stop managing your Snowflake environment — start architecting it. This session shows how to master complex, multi-account ecosystems at scale without administrative overhead. Explore how Organizations 2.0 enables multi-account visibility and control, and how the Well-Architected Framework drives platform health through automated blueprints and health checks. Discover the latest innovations for precise cost governance and see how Cortex AI transforms administration with intelligent automation to standardize best practices and streamline enterprise operations.

title: ACCELERATING T.ROWE PRICE’S DATA PRODUCT STRATEGY WITH MODERN MDM ON SNOWFLAKE
description: As T.Rowe Price grew its $1.78 trillion asset management business with new solutions, partners and enhanced digital + AI capabilities, the legacy master data management (MDM) became a bottleneck. The “trust and latency” gap eroded confidence from business and executives while it increased TCO due to complex ETL pipelines, manual rework and unmanageable technical debt. In this session, T.Rowe Price will share their data product strategy vision and journey of migrating legacy MDM into a cloud-native “Party Master 360” using Semarchy on Snowflake.
speaker_title: Head of Enterprise Data Solution - Cheif Data Office

title: AGENT-TO-AGENT DATA SUPPLY CHAINS: DELIVERING DATA AT MACHINE SCALE
description: Snowflake Intelligence provides answers quickly, but data availability latency is the limiting factor. Traditional platforms analyze existing data, they don't respond when new data is needed. When an analyst or AI needs data not in the warehouse, manual source integration, pipeline building and transformation adaptation introduce delays AI can't bypass. This session reveals agent-to-agent coordination: Snowflake Intelligence spots data gaps, and Maia operates upstream to discover sources, auto-generate pipelines, reconcile schemas and deliver queryable datasets in minutes, without manual work.

title: BLUE YONDER: ACCELERATING BUSINESS TRANSFORMATION WITH AGENTIC AI
description: Blue Yonder embarked on a business transformation journey two years ago with a goal to complete the journey by Q1 2027. The goal is to change from a traditional ISV software company that has been in business for over 30 years into a best-of-breed SaaS provider, modeled after the Snowflake operating model. Chris Burchett, SVP of Generative AI at Blue Yonder, speak's about the company's journey and focuses on how it utilizes agentic technology — from Snowflake Cortex AI, to value-sell Blue Yonder's legacy on-premises customers to move, to its AI-enabled SaaS AI-driven supply chain platform.

title: HOW AI ACCELERATES IDEAS TO RESULTS WITH SNOWFLAKE NOTEBOOKS
description: In this session, we showcase the latest AI capabilities in Snowflake Notebooks, which are integrated directly with Cortex Code. Learn how admins can leverage Cortex Code for unified notebooks governance and data teams can be 10x more productive in writing/reviewing code and delivering results and business impact.

title: PAYPAL: MERCHANT FEE PREDICTIONS IN SNOWFLAKE ML
description: At PayPal, third-party providers could predict interchange and scheme fees but granular transaction-level fees were arriving only at the end of the month. This would take one month for most cases and three days in best-case scenarios with an error-prone process. In this session, PayPal engineers explain how PayPal built a modern near real-time AI/ML pipeline in Snowflake to accurately predict merchant fees daily at the transaction level. Learn how this accelerates settlement times, saves costs and enables faster and more expansive merchant acquisition.

title: PROVIDENCE HEALTH: HOW DATA MESH ARCHITECTURE UNLOCKED AN AI-POWERED FUTURE
description: Over the past six years, Providence Health & Services has transformed the data and analytics strategy across the enterprise, implementing a data mesh architecture that focuses on a governed and trusted data foundation. This foundation is the crucial start to unlocking and accelerating value to be gained from expanding self-service analytics, AI capabilities and application development. Come hear Arek Kaczmarek, AVP of Data Engineering, Health Intelligence, and Kellie Larkin, VP of Identity Access Management, Enterprise Information & Security, as they share the journey to today and the path ahead.

title: UNLOCKING RAG ON COMPLEX DOCUMENTS: LANDINGAI MEETS SNOWFLAKE INTELLIGENCE
description: You can stand up a RAG pipeline in an afternoon. Getting it to work on real documents — dense tables, scientific figures and irregular layouts  — takes months. This session cuts that gap. We'll walk through an end-to-end workflow using LandingAI's Agentic Document Extraction to parse complex, multimodal content and extract structured key-value pairs that break conventional tools. From there, you'll see how parsed output feeds the Snowflake Cortex AI stack — Cortex Search, Cortex Analyst and Cortex Agents — all surfaced through Snowflake Intelligence. The entire stack is created with one SQL script and can be versioned for your own use case in about half a day.

title: WHAT'S NEW: POWER REAL-TIME APPS AND AI WITHOUT COMPLEX DATA MOVEMENT
description: Stop wrestling with pipelines to move data between transactional and analytical silos. Discover how Snowflake is unifying these worlds into a single, high-performance platform where your data is seamlessly connected and ready for action. We’ll dive into the latest breakthroughs in Snowflake Postgres and Unistore that allow you to build responsive, real-time apps and AI experiences without the friction of complex ETL. Join us to see live demos and hear from customers who are already leveraging Snowflake to reduce latency, slash architectural complexity and ship AI-native products faster than ever.
speakers: Christopher Heim; Mike Willett; Phillip Coletti

title: WHAT'S NEW: UNIFY YOUR LAKEHOUSE WITH SNOWFLAKE SIMPLE INTEROPERABILITY
description: Pipeline sprawl and unnecessary data movement are the hidden taxes to your AI innovation. Discover how Snowflake’s platform empowers you to work from a single governed copy of data, wherever it lives. Built on native support for Apache Polaris™ and Apache Iceberg™, this session explores how to power new use cases with Iceberg V3 support and virtually eliminate storage complexity with Snowflake Storage, all without vendor lock-in. We'll also cover strategies to reduce governance and security risks by building a metadata-driven universal governance model to fuel human and agentic intelligence with access to governed, live data.

title: WHAT’S NEW: ZERO TO A TRUSTED SEMANTIC LAYER IN SNOWFLAKE
description: Stop struggling with fragmented logic and start building a trusted data foundation in minutes. Join us to see how easy it is to create Snowflake Semantic Views that turn raw data into governed, actionable insights. We’ll demonstrate how our connected ecosystem allows your business logic to flow seamlessly from your data cloud to your AI agents and BI tools. This session will feature a live demo of our latest easy-to-build semantic innovations.

title: SNOWBASH
description: Join the Summit community at Snowbash! It's an early evening gathering to connect, unwind and celebrate the day. Enjoy music and conversation in a vibrant setting designed to carry the momentum forward.

title: Training // Track 3 - Snowflake Platform Training + Exam - Thursday
description: This session is designed for individuals with limited experience using Snowflake and provides a walkthrough of the AI Data Cloud architecture through interactive labs, guiding learners how to unify, integrate and share previously siloed data with virtually unlimited scalability and concurrency.

title: A Seamless Path to Managing at Scale with Organizations 2.0
description: Snowflake’s Orgs 2.0 shifts to an organization-first model, enabling scalable multi-account management for the AI Data Cloud. It provides org-level visibility, unified security and streamlined data governance to simplify your Snowflake footprint. This session covers key innovations: AI-backed control with Org Hub and Command Center; enhanced security through centralized governance and proactive access monitoring; improved cost control via Org Usage Views, Org Tags and Budgets (private preview); and seamless migration using guided wizards and Coco skills for Org Users and Groups.

title: Best Practices for Snowflake-Managed MCP Setup and Integration
description: The introduction of the Snowflake-managed MCP server has generated significant customer interest and practical deployment questions. This session directly addresses the most common challenges we've observed across numerous customer engagements, specifically focusing on server setup, secure authentication, tool integration and establishing external client connectivity. Attendees will gain practical guidance and validated best practices derived from real-world deployments to ensure a seamless and successful MCP implementation.

title: Beyond the Landing Zone: From Raw Streams and Files to Governed Iceberg Tables
description: Apache Iceberg is the standard for data sovereignty and interoperability, but building a high-performance lakehouse is often slowed by complex engineering. Modern data is fragmented across real-time streams, CDC and cloud files. Manually transforming these into a performant Iceberg architecture requires massive effort to manage maintenance, metadata and partitioning. This session shows how Snowflake acts as a universal engine for Iceberg. Learn how Snowflake automates the data lifecycle — from ingestion to continuous optimization — without manual intervention. See how to transform any raw source into governed, production-ready Iceberg tables that stay open and easy to manage at scale.

title: Braze Operator AI: Automating Business Reviews with Snowflake Cortex AI
description: Braze's customer analytics team built an AI agent on Snowflake Cortex AI to automate data insights for its GTM teams — eliminating manual analysis bottlenecks. Leveraging Cortex Agents, Snowflake Intelligence and declarative sharing, the solution drives new revenue for the business through self-service analytics packages, strengthens sales propositions by delivering immediate value to stakeholders, and powers vertical-specific GTM playbooks. Nontechical teams at Braze gain Day 1 access to actionable insights — no data engineers required.

title: Build a Universal Semantic Layer for BI and AI: Hands-On Lab
description: This 90-minute hands-on lab demonstrates how to create a semantic layer in Snowflake using semantic views. Attendees will learn to leverage features such as Semantic View Autopilot and Cortex Code to accelerate the creation of a governed data model. You will build a single source of truth that can be accessed by traditional BI tools and used in tandem with Snowflake Cortex AI to enable the fast deployment of complex, robust chatbots and AI tools.

title: Building Open Source Native Apps: Johnson & Johnson's Drug Discovery Solution
description: Join this session to learn how research teams at Johnson & Johnson (J&J) are implementing an RDKit chemical library on Snowflake — unlocking the power of high-speed cheminformatics to accelerate downstream drug discovery. Hear how J&J's IT teams are reducing operational complexity with zero data movement by leveraging advanced programming techniques through Snowpark for Python stored procedures and UDFs. Discover how J&J and Snowflake are partnering to make J&J's solution available to the broader healthcare and life sciences community as an open source Snowflake Native App on Snowflake Marketplace.

title: Dev Day Opening
description: Join us as Snowflake's CEO, Sridhar Ramaswamy, delivers opening remarks.
room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

title: Open Semantic Interchange: Dimensions and Ontologies for Agents
description: As data, BI and AI converge, enterprises need a shared semantic foundation that works across tools, models and workloads. This session introduces Open Semantic Interchange (OSI) — a minimal, expressive metadata standard built on three primitives: concepts, relationships and relational formulas. OSI supports both dimensional BI models and ontological application semantics, enabling interoperability across ecosystems like dbt, Legend, Palantir, custom applications and BI dashboards. We’ll show how OSI enables a durable way to define semantics once and use them everywhere, while insulating models from schema drift and accelerating cross-system integration in Snowflake.

title: Real-Time Lead Prioritization with Snowflake Model Serving Boosts Podium Sales
description: Discover how Podium turned call-level data into real-time lead recommendations entirely inside Snowflake ML. Using the Snowflake Feature Store, we assembled a leakage-safe data set, trained an XGBoost model on a GPU compute pool and registered it in the Snowflake Model Registry — then deployed it through Snowpark Inference Services for real-time scoring. Our business app queries the REST API to prioritize outreach instantly, leveraging engagement momentum, appointment readiness and temporal patterns — driving measurable increases in lead conversion and showing how live, data-driven scoring can accelerate sales outcomes for any business.

title: The Age of Data Exhaustion
description:
No one talks about it, but data teams are tired. They're tired of managing thousands of tables, brittle transformation logic and AI pilots built on foundations no one fully trusts. The problem isn’t data volume. It’s overload. Code overload demands disciplined transformation. Data overload demands intelligent cataloging. And chaos erodes trust.

In this session, we’ll show how modern data teams restore order with a metadata-driven data operating layer that standardizes transformation, connects governance to build-time and makes data quality proactive instead of reactive. Because AI doesn’t fix disorder, it amplifies it.
room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

title: Best Practices for Protecting and Auditing Sensitive Data in an AI-First World
description: In the AI-first world, privacy and compliance are nonnegotiable necessities, not optional add-ons. The rapid proliferation of sensitive data, coupled with the increasing reliance on AI and machine learning models, makes it critical to proactively address regulations like HIPAA, PCI-DSS, CCPA and GDPR. Failure to do so not only risks massive regulatory fines but also erodes customer trust. This makes privacy and compliance a strategic priority powered by intelligent automation. In this session, learn about how Snowflake addresses this challenge with Horizon Catalog's Sensitive Data Classification and new AI-driven capabilities for data governance and audit.

title: Beyond Passwords: Strong Authentication for Snowflake Workloads
description: While strong authentication is essential for accessing Snowflake resources, implementing it across all users and services can be complex. This session will detail real-world customer transitions from traditional methods (passwords, keys) to robust, modern authentication solutions like Workload Identity Federation (secretless authentication). We will explore effective rollout strategies and migration procedures, enabling you to learn the latest techniques for modernizing authentication without disrupting critical workloads.

title: From Data Chaos to Clarity: The Intelligent Data Marketplace Blueprint
description: Every enterprise is racing to turn data into intelligence, yet most struggle with fragmented tools and inconsistent governance. This session shares how we built an Intelligent Internal Marketplace powered by Snowflake Horizon Catalog for trust and governance and Cortex Code for AI-driven discovery. Learn how automated tagging, lineage and intelligent search make data instantly findable, reliable and ready for action. Attendees will explore a practical framework and real-world lessons to unify governance, AI and discovery — driving adoption, data literacy and faster, trusted business outcomes.

title: How to Give Snowflake Intelligence the Customer Context It's Missing
description: Snowflake Intelligence is transforming how teams interact with data. But insight quality depends on the data underneath. Snowplow's customer context layer delivers validated, enriched behavioral data into Snowflake with prebuilt semantic models that accelerate Snowflake Cortex Analyst, letting anyone self-serve insights without the data team. Crucially, Snowplow captures every digital interaction, with a human and AI agent alike, so teams can distinguish and optimize for both within the same Snowflake Cortex AI queries. The same context layer powering internal analytics also fuels customer-facing AI agents, creating a compounding intelligence loop where every interaction sharpens every insight.
room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

title: OpenTelemetry and Iceberg: Foundation for Observability at Scale
description: AI-driven systems generate massive volumes of metrics, logs and traces with extreme cardinality and long retention needs. Traditional observability backends struggle to scale, govern and control costs. In this session, learn how Observe uses Snowflake as the backbone of an open observability architecture built on OpenTelemetry and Apache Iceberg. We’ll show how telemetry flows from OTel-instrumented apps through collectors into user’s own Iceberg data lakehouse, enabling open access, strong governance and analytics at petabyte scale. This approach lowers storage and query costs while delivering faster incident detection and resolution — without vendor lock-in.

title: Real-Time Openflow Oracle CDC for Enterprise with Yes Energy's Story
description: Oracle holds the most critical enterprise data and getting it into Snowflake in real time is the defining Oracle data engineering challenge. Every DBA asks before enabling CDC: will this hurt the database? We answer with data. Jakub Puchalski from Snowflake benchmarks XStream at 37,000+ TPS quantifying redo volume, CPU and commit latency. Dan Goodman from Yes Energy demos an Exadata deployment: billion-row CDC tables in production and a real recovery story that proves what "resilient by design" means. Join this session for a DBA-ready architecture blueprint, a checklist to protect your OLTP system, and confidence to move your most critical Oracle workloads to Snowflake Openflow.

title: Roche's Data Mesh Strategy: Governance, Observability and AI in Pharma
description: F. Hoffmann-La Roche, the world's leading provider of cancer treatments and Europe's third largest company by market cap, is building a cutting-edge data platform on Snowflake. This session explores Roche's approach to data mesh with mandatory data governance through object tagging, Snowflake Cortex AI integration including MCP server, and data observability supporting Roche's data mesh architecture. Learn how Roche is implementing these capabilities across its pharmaceutical operations, including through real-world use case deep dives, success stories and the company's technical domain roadmap.
room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

title: Zero-ETL Citizen Development on Snowflake: Apps with Postgres and SPCS
description: Business teams need to build applications faster while keeping their data ready for analytics and AI. This session presents a modern application stack on Snowflake that connects applications and data on a single platform. It explores how Snowflake Postgres and Snowpark Container Services enable new application patterns, and how open-source ecosystems and multiple technical communities — PostgreSQL, low-code OSS and the Snowflake community — can collaborate to accelerate innovation. The session highlights Snowflake’s evolution beyond analytics into a unified platform for applications, data and community-driven development.

title: Accelerating AI-Ready Data with Open Semantic Interchange
description: As data, BI and AI converge, enterprises need a shared semantic foundation that works across tools, models, and workloads. Explore how the Open Semantic Interchange (OSI) creates a vendor-neutral semantic standard that lets BI, analytics and AI tools speak the same language for reliable, consistent data interpretation. This session will explore how OSI supports dimensional BI models, enabling a durable way to define semantics once and use them everywhere. Learn how this standard insulates your models from schema drift and accelerates cross-system integration in Snowflake, ensuring your traditional BI dashboards and new AI applications always agree on the numbers.

title: Batch Inference on Multimodal Models
description: Join this session to learn how Snowflake ML enables teams to deploy multimodal models — from Hugging Face or custom builds — directly within Snowflake. By natively supporting image, audio and text, organizations can run large-scale inference on unstructured data without managing complex infrastructure.

title: Best Practices for Building Real-Time AI Experiences with Snowflake Streaming
description: Agentic AI applications demand continuous and up-to-date data to make autonomous decisions and deliver personalized experiences. But building reliable real-time pipelines remains one of the hardest problems in data engineering. In this session, learn how Snowflake's streaming capabilities including Snowpipe Streaming, Dynamic Tables and native Kafka integration provide a simple, scalable and cost-effective foundation for real-time AI workloads. We'll walk through common architecture patterns and real customer examples so you can start building real-time AI applications today.

title: From Vision to Intelligence: How Cortex AI Accelerates FSFB’s AI Journey
description: Join Fundación Santa Fe de Bogotá (FSFB), a top-250 global hospital, to see how it is empowering clinicians with generative AI. This session explores FSFB's NEXUS engine, built on Snowflake, which interprets and structures complex clinical data. By leveraging Snowflake Cortex Search, FSFB has enabled doctors to "chat with the EHR" to get instant patient summaries in the ER and identify cancer suspicions. This early detection saves lives by allowing for more effective, less aggressive treatments with better outcomes. The result: Processing 5,000 clinical records now takes six hours, not 583. AI doesn't replace doctors; it empowers their ability to save lives.
room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

title: How DTCC Enabled AI-Driven Data Cataloguing at Scale with Cortex and Streamlit
description: Scaling intelligent metadata tagging across a modern data estate is one of the most persistent challenges in enterprise data management. This session demonstrates how the Depository Trust and Clearing Corporation (DTCC) used Snowflake Cortex AI and Streamlit to automate the creation, approval and management of business definitions from structured and unstructured data. Learn how DTCC eliminated manual cataloguing bottlenecks with scalable architecture, intuitive workflows and an AI-powered hub that drives metadata consistency and visibility across the enterprise — marking their first gen AI application in production and driving $1.2 million in cost avoidance.

title: What’s New: Innovate at Scale: Powering a Secure and Resilient AI Estate
description: This session unveils Snowflake innovations designed to protect sensitive AI assets and ensure continuity. Explore what’s new in security for AI, featuring capabilities to secure Cortex flows, Snowflake Intelligence and Cortex Code. We’ll cover AI security posture in the Trust Center, adaptive guardrails against prompt injection and sandboxing to mitigate local risks. Learn about new data movement policies to prevent exfiltration and seamless agent access via agentic identity and JIT provisioning as well as Account Replication 2.0, the next evolution of our BCDR engine. Join us to see these features in action and build a bulletproof, AI-ready estate.
speakers: Radhika Janardanan; Sid Ramadoss; Nitika Gupta
room: Basecamp South Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

title: Banking on Uptime: Morgan Stanley Achieves Resilience at Scale with Snowflake
description: In an always-on economy, resilience is nonnegotiable for mission-critical data platforms. This session explores how Snowflake delivers enterprise-grade disaster recovery and business continuity — from failover groups to account replication, client redirect, and immutable backups that safeguard against cloud outages and ransomware attacks while meeting compliance mandates. Snowflake experts will share the latest innovations in resilience and best practices, followed by a deep dive from Morgan Stanley on how they architect resilience at global scale to meet stringent regulatory, RTO and RPO requirements.

title: Build Real-Time User Analytics with Snowpipe Streaming and Cortex Agents
description: Stop relying on stale dashboards and build your own live user analytics engine. In this session, we will architect a scalable user analytics platform end-to-end using Snowpipe Streaming. Discover how to use Snowflake CLI and Cortex AI to automate pipeline code generation, deploying elastic channels for effortless auto-scaling and error tables for native dead-letter queue handling. We will demonstrate how to go from raw clickstream events to AI-driven insights in minutes, removing the operational complexity of traditional streaming stacks.

title: How Capital One Architected for Interoperability in a Multi-Catalog/Table Setup
description: As one of Snowflake's earliest and largest adopters, Capital One has continually driven innovation in cloud data management, focusing on optimizing cost and governance at a massive scale. This session details our strategic journey toward a Bring Your Own Compute model, illustrating how we evolved our architecture to meet rigorous security, governance and cost optimization requirements in a federated data environment. A core component of this evolution was implementing a sophisticated architecture that allows data consumers flexibility while maintaining central control via multi-catalog and multi-table format architecture, achieving data residency and compliance, and maximizing finops efficiency.

title: Millions Saved: Unity's Native Apps, Data Sharing and AI Strategy
description: Learn how Unity, known for its industry-leading game engine, transformed its analytics platform with Snowflake, delivering seamless data and app sharing with cost-efficient scalability. Discover how Unity's native application solution saved millions annually and enhanced customer experience. See how it's incorporating Cortex Agents with Streamlit to enable conversational analytics, helping customers maximize the value of their data. Walk away with insights on building native apps that deliver customer value while driving significant cost savings.
room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

title: Modernizing Banking: From Legacy Spark to Near Real Time with Snowflake Openflow
description: UnionBank Philippines’ Quickloans workflows were constrained by legacy Informatica BDS and Cloudera (Apache Spark), blocking Cloudera decommissioning. By refactoring and migrating these complex pipelines to Snowflake Openflow’s native constructs, the team fully preserved business rules while materially boosting processing speed. The new design proved stable with low latency during peak windows (for example, paydays), outperforming the previous solution. This outcome validated retiring Cloudera and enabled a full move to a scalable, cloud-native Snowflake data architecture.

title: What Apache Iceberg Is Bad at Doing … for Now
description: Apache Iceberg is rapidly becoming the open source table standard, but popularity doesn’t mean everyone is using it efficiently. The format has some strong features — ACID, metric pushdowns and hidden partitioning — but it also has some dangerous anti-patterns. In this talk we’ll go over bloated metadata, inefficient metrics and missing maintenance bogging down query planning. You’ll learn why these issues plague users of Iceberg tables and how to avoid them. Finally, we’ll also go through how some of these problems won’t even be an issue in version 4 with new proposals like “single file commits.” Come and learn to avoid pitfalls like a pro and get a preview of what’s coming next!

title: Write Once, Enforce Everywhere: Interoperable Governance with Iceberg
description:
Interoperable governance means enforcing consistent row- and column-level security across all engines without fragmenting policies. How do you achieve this when engines differ? This talk presents two approaches via Apache Iceberg:


	Catalog-enforced FGAC using scan planning to return policy-enforced files
	ReadRestrictions standardization where catalogs return policy expressions engines interpreted for deeper integration


You'll learn catalog-side architecture, including scan planning and policy evaluation. You'll also learn about two paths: transparent enforcement vs ReadRestrictions standardization — and trade-offs around interoperable governance.

title: Snowflake Startup Challenge Finale
description: Experience the 2026 Snowflake Startup Challenge Finale, held during Snowflake Dev Day in San Francisco. This annual competition draws hundreds of early-stage startups Powered by Snowflake, all vying to fuel AI innovation, gain global exposure and compete for up to $1 million in investments from Snowflake Ventures. Now in its sixth year, the Snowflake Startup Challenge — sponsored in partnership with the New York Stock Exchange — continues to spotlight and invest in the most innovative startups building on Snowflake. Join us as the top three finalists present their groundbreaking AI-driven solutions to an expert panel of judges, leading to the exciting announcement of this year’s grand prize winner.
room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

title: Build an AI Agent with Streamlit and Postgres
description: In this hands-on lab, we put you in the driver’s seat of the modern AI application stack. Together we will build a powerful, data-friendly Python application using just two core technologies: Streamlit’s “pure Python" approach for a rapid, beautiful frontend, and Postgres for robust data handling and semantic search. We will build a functional AI agent that allows users to query data using Snowflake Cortex AI. You will learn how to build a Streamlit interface, connect to an agent, and add interactive, data-driven elements like charts, graphs and maps. You’ll leave this session with the tools to unlock value by turning existing data and static text into an interactive AI agent.

title: How Canva Uses Event-Driven APIs and Scalable Pipelines with Snowflake Openflow
description:
Polling for data is inefficient, and manual resource management breaks at scale. This deep dive, co-presented wtih Canva, explores how Snowflake Openflow evolves into a programmable layer of the AI Data Cloud. We'll show how to design event-based flows with Snowflake Openflow Endpoints, a new capability that exposes listeners to handle inbound events.

Participants will learn how to design "push-based" architectures that react instantly, backed by the new Snowflake Openflow APIs. We'll demonstrate how to programmatically manage the lifecycle of Runtimes and Connectors using the new API. Join us to build a system that's not only event-driven but fully deployable via code.

title: Interoperable Lakehouse 101: Single Governed Data Copy for AI Readiness
description: Stop the data silo and fragmentation tax. In this introductory hands-on lab, you will learn how to work on a single governed data copy that slashes costs and engineering effort. You will create Apache Iceberg tables in Snowflake Storage, apply Snowflake Horizon governance and secure multi-engine, and build Semantic Views to use AI to query your lakehouse with natural language. Achieve true data autonomy over your data and make your architecture AI-ready.

title: Powering Production ML at Scale with Snowflake Feature Store
description: Production ML relies on features that are consistent, governed and available in real time. In this session, we share what’s new in Snowflake Feature Store and how teams productionize large-scale ML with minimal operational overhead. We'll highlight new product capabilities that simplify feature reuse and improve freshness, reliability and performance improvement around efficient aggregations. You will hear about Attentive's customer journey migrating its feature store to Snowflake for streamlined production-grade ML efficiently across the organization at scale.

title: Scale Intelligence, Not Infrastructure: Unify Your Data Estate with Microsoft
description: Stop managing infrastructure and start scaling intelligence. Data silos shouldn't hold your AI initiatives and business insights hostage. In this session, you’ll learn how Microsoft and Snowflake make it easy to unify your data estate with native integrations that embrace interoperability, powering your analytics, AI and business applications — without complex infrastructure maintenance. We’ll showcase the latest advancements in connecting Snowflake with Power BI, Microsoft Fabric, Microsoft 365 Copilot and Teams to fuel everyday workflows, enhance productivity and drive business transformation.
room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

title: The Optima Vision: Making it Impossible to Run Inefficiently on Snowflake
description: Snowflake Optima is our new optimization engine that represents a paradigm shift in effortless performance.
