It leverages deep intelligence, automatically analyzes your workload history and delivers intelligent optimizations that automatically accelerate performance. The result: industry-leading performance, made even simpler. This session dives deep into how Optima automatically optimizes your workloads and announces exciting new Optima features.
room: Basecamp South Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

title: Building the Block Data MCP: A Blueprint for Gen AI Insights and Analytics
description: Your data warehouse holds the answers to your most important business questions. But without an AI-ready data architecture, the LLM can’t access or use them. We solved this problem at Block by building the Block Data MCP, combining a governed semantic layer, a Snowflake client and AI agents (Goose), to map business questions to trusted metrics, layered with generative AI insights. In this session we share how we designed and implemented the underlying architecture, eliminated AI hallucinations through deterministic data flows, and scaled our Block Data MCP to support ~2,000 internal data users and various business use cases.

title: From Customer to Co‑Creator: Aviv’s Role in Shaping dbt Projects on Snowflake
description: When a KKR‑backed European classifieds group committed to rebuilding its data platform in under six months, it also reframed Snowflake as a co‑builder rather than a vendor. Aviv and Snowflake formed a joint squad of product managers, engineers and data practitioners focused on dbt Projects on Snowflake. Aviv gained faster, cleaner delivery of five revenue‑critical data products and a single governed way of working. Attendees will see how Snowflake gained a demanding early adopter whose real‑world requirements and design reviews directly shaped the maturity, usability and roadmap of dbt Projects.

title: Preventing the Snowball Effect: Morgan Stanley’s 10 Principles for Scale FinOps
description: Unchecked consumption can quietly compound into runaway spend. Morgan Stanley shares 10 guiding principles that prevent cost snowballing while enabling innovation. Learn how governance guardrails, real-time monitoring and cross-functional accountability create sustainable cloud economics at enterprise scale.

title: What’s New: Build and Deploy Apps at Lightning Speed
description: Bring your code to your data, and go from ideas to apps in minutes. Snowflake Apps is evolving to bridge the gap between your code and the enterprise data ecosystem. We’ll unveil the launch of native support for modern frontend frameworks like React and showcase new integrations with AI-powered development workflows. Discover how to move from a natural language prompt to a secure, production-ready app in record time, and hear from companies that are already building faster on Snowflake.
room: Basecamp South Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

title: What's New in Snowflake AI Research
description: Snowflake AI Research is focused on delivering high-quality, efficient AI for real enterprise workloads — bridging state-of-the-art research with practical product impact. In this session, we’ll share what’s new across our research agenda, highlighting key directions including model-agent co-design to improve task success and reliability, vertical models specialized for enterprise use cases, and advanced training and inference systems that enhance usability and performance while reducing cost and latency. We’ll also share principles and lessons learned, and how research transitions into production to help teams build trustworthy AI applications on governed data at scale.

title: Apache Polaris in Practice: Open Catalogs, Open Formats, Open Community
description: Learn how Apache Polaris is becoming the open catalog layer for every engine and format. Engineers from Snowflake and other organizations will walk through new capabilities including S3-compatible on-prem storage, generic tables, a built-in policy store, audit logs and events, catalog federation and the evolving Polaris REST catalog spec. A customer and a partner will demonstrate how they use and contribute to Polaris, including Delta, Lance format support beyond Iceberg and concrete ways you can get involved.

title: Build REST APIs for your Data Product with Snowpark Container Services
description: Data products evolve, and so must their APIs. This session focuses on how Snowflake SPCS empowers developers to host and run data applications, including custom services, directly alongside their data. The core theme is offering your data applications as seamless REST APIs to consumers. We'll show a practical example of wrapping a data product in an API and allowing partners or customers to consume the service instantly via standard HTTP requests, completely bypassing the need for installing connectors and drivers. This approach drastically simplifies consumption and opens new avenues for data monetization and Secure Data Sharing.
room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

title: GROW YOUR CAREER IN THE AGE OF AI - AMA
description: The workforce is at a breaking point. Between a volatile tech market and the rise of autonomous AI, the traditional career ladder has been shifting and considered a moving target. But disruption is the greatest catalyst for growth. We are entering the agentic era — a frontier where AI doesn't just assist but acts. This session explores how industry veterans and newcomers alike can stop playing defense, master the art of AI collaboration and pivot their skill sets to thrive in the new economy. Where there is disruption there is massive opportunity. This is the moment to reskill and pivot to meet the demands of the rapidly expanding landscape created by the agentic era. Join Snowflake’s Chief People Officer, Arnnon Geshuri, as he leads a cross-disciplinary panel of luminaries covering venture capital, academia and the economy. They share their insights and answer your questions in an interactive and candid AMA.

title: MAKING MEDICINE COMPUTABLE: SCALING MULTIMODAL FOUNDATION MODELS ON SNOWFLAKE
description: We introduce APOLLO, a multimodal clinical foundation model trained on 25 billion longitudinal records from Mass General Brigham for advanced similarity search and outcome prediction. Aevius will showcase a Snowflake Native App that generates APOLLO embeddings within a client’s secure environment — enabling hospitalwide search without exposing PHI. This deployment provides a roadmap for enterprise intelligence, using agentic workflows and reasoning to automate complex analytical tasks.
speakers: Long Le; Rowland Pettit; Murali Gandhirajan Gandhirajan
speaker_title: MGH Vice Chair for Pathology Informatics

title: ONBOARDING AI: FROM BUSINESS AMBIGUITY TO AI ACCURACY WITH SEMANTIC MODELS
description: AI initiatives often stall not because of algorithms but because underlying data lacks structure, meaning and governance. Without a semantic foundation, AI agents and analytics tools struggle to interpret business context. In this session, we explore how semantic modeling bridges the gap between business meaning and AI-ready data. Learn how modern data teams use structured semantic layers, governed data models and collaborative design workflows to turn ambiguous business questions into accurate AI outputs, enabling trusted analytics, reliable AI agents and scalable data products on Snowflake.

title: PAGERDUTY USES SNOWFLAKE CORTEX AI FOR EFFICIENT, CONSISTENT CUSTOMER ENGAGEMENT
description: PagerDuty, in collaboration with Snowflake Professional Services, has transformed how its leadership, sales and field teams access information and make decisions, thanks to the implementation of Snowflake Cortex AI services. The solution leverages Cortex Agents, Cortex Analyst and Cortex Search to gather data from multiple sources on the customer's next best action, contact information and recommended next steps. This information is then concisely summarized and presented in a unified, actionable format. PagerDuty estimates this initiative will result in multi-million-dollar savings while ensuring organizational consistency in customer interactions throughout the customer lifecycle.

title: TURNING AI PREDICTIONS INTO REAL-WORLD WATER SOLUTIONS
description: Water is our most vital resource, yet its quality remains a global puzzle. Using some of the 26 million data points from the UN Environment Programme, participants of the 2026 EY AI and Data Challenge developed ML models to uncover the environmental drivers of water quality. But how do we move from a challenge competition to real-world usability? This panel explores the commercialization roadmap for water-quality AI. We address:

title: ZERO PII, FULL INSIGHTS: CORTEX AI TRANSFORMS INSURANCE CLAIMS PROCESSING
description: How do you unlock analytics from claims data packed with sensitive PII? Global Excel Management leverages Cortex and Horizon Catalog's AI_REDACT to automatically detect and redact personal information at scale. Join us to see our real-world architecture, chunking strategies for long documents, and how we combined AI_REDACT with other Cortex functions to build a compliant, insight-rich claims pipeline. Learn practical strategies for chunking long documents, handling edge cases and building a privacy-first data pipeline that satisfies regulators while unlocking AI-driven insights.

title: 3 ESSENTIALS FOR BUILDING WINNING AI PROJECTS
description: Many AI projects fail not because of models but because the data lacks context. AI success relies on knowing where data lives, what it means and whether it is fresh. Without enterprise context, applications and agents fail. Join Cloudera and Sanjeev Mohan, Analyst at SanjMo, to explore three essentials to give AI the context it needs:

title: BUILDING AN END-TO-END AI APPLICATION ON SNOWFLAKE: FROM DATA TO INTELLIGENCE
description: Build a complete AI retail app entirely in Snowflake. Start by using Cortex Code to generate SQL and dbt models for metrics such as CLV. Next, train a GPU-accelerated XGBoost model in Snowflake Notebooks, register it, and deploy it as a service. To handle OLTP, use Snowflake Postgres and sync data to Apache Iceberg on S3, demonstrating lakehouse interoperability with pg_lake. Finally, unify the stack with Snowflake Intelligence, a conversational interface orchestrating Snowflake Cortex Analyst, Snowflake Cortex Search and your ML models for a seamless analytical experience.
speakers: Dash Desai Desai; Arun Agarwal Agarwal

title: THE AUTONOMY PARADOX: WILL HUMANS ALWAYS BE IN THE LOOP?
description: The industry is racing toward fully autonomous agents, yet enterprise deployments keep hitting the same barrier: production-grade confidence. Is full autonomy really the end goal, or will human-in-the-loop remain a permanent and essential layer of enterprise AI? And beyond autonomy, what other paths can give enterprises the confidence required for production use?

title: FROM PROMPTS TO PIPELINES: THE RISE OF THE AGENTIC HARNESS
description: For the last two years, the developer community has been obsessed with "prompt engineering." But as AI scales into production enterprise systems, the prompt is now only 10% of the work. The remaining 90% is the harness — the engineering pipelines, tool orchestrations and execution sandboxes that allow an agent to take multistep autonomous action. In this luminary talk, Romain explores why the next generation of builders must pivot from "prompting" to "agentX (agent experience) engineering." Drawing on his front-row view of world-class agent deployments, he provides a high-level architectural overview of the agentic harness, touching on the critical shift toward persistent memory, secure execution environments and automated evaluation loops. Attendees will walk away with a high-level blueprint for transitioning from building fragile chatbots to architecting robust, enterprise-grade agentic pipelines.

title: PAVING THE ROADS FOR AGENTS: REBUILDING INFRASTRUCTURE FOR THE AUTONOMOUS ERA
description: Dropping an autonomous AI agent into a traditional software pipeline is like putting a sports car on a dirt road — it will inevitably break down. Today's enterprise tech stacks were built exclusively for human engineers. So what happens when your most active code contributors become machines? In this session, Factory's CEO, Matan Grinberg, explores the fundamental infrastructure shift required to support an autonomous engineering workforce. Moving beyond simple code generation, Matan discusses the industry-wide necessity of "paving the roads" for agentic systems. He illustrates how the entire software development lifecycle (SDLC) must evolve, offering builders a practical framework for transitioning to agent-native environments. Attendees learn how to establish the deterministic guardrails, AI-readable APIs and robust data foundations necessary for autonomous systems to build, test and deploy at full speed.

title: WINNING WHEN CODE IS FREE: HOW DEVELOPERS BUILD AI STARTUPS THAT SCALE
description: As coding costs approach zero and technical moats disappear, the advantage shifts from engineering to strategy. This talk shows developers how to build companies that win through product-led growth, distribution, network effects and community integration, and how these factors align with what investors look for in scalable AI startups.
